name: Communists in YOUR City? [URGENT]
subject: We couldn’t believe it either, Friend...
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj206vqmw8e0tuopuncep0tbs2251pefzaemcnustraocloah6jk9ck8dnooj04yx82svqw60wq712642q0pi&__stmp=t0q9h2&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1ekudaep13hzde9a4yblo6mdlz6e8ur9ft8ookuzgpxpuk4ln4lvs66pfluzmgg21y00keenz652t22cehl5fqdconodlqfqnvj5o2vedo9z0uf4m106zdmbl7spcg45cc407d33tj&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1ekudaep13hzde9a4yblo6mdlz6e8ur9ft8ookuzgpxpuk4ln4lvs66pfluzmgg21y00keenz652t22cehl5fqdconodlqfqnvj5o2vedo9z0uf4m106zdmbl7spcg45cc407d33tj&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t0q9h2&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

subject: Reminder: 🚨 Your Weekly Update From The NEGOP
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY](https://feyrpzhbb
body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---
As a proud registered Republican, you are the heartbeat of Nebraska's conservative movement--and we need you now more than ever.
Our revamped NEGOP+ Booster Club is your opportunity to support our mission throughout the year. In the past, a few large donors carried the load, but now it's grassroots supporters like you who are making the real difference. Monthly contributions of $5, $25, or more provide a stable foundation to fund voter outreach, train candidates, and defend our values across the state.
When you join, you'll also receive exclusive perks like custom NEGOP gear, VIP event access, and insider updates from party leaders. Join today at [https://ne.gop/negop/ ](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001WBcVsLipe3AxgUx18l70OIfZSE2XqnADFmVoFJfwynAxBSFS5NN46B76jnkTDDrwshu7roxw9Ch1ckPZY-FSc4AEtAbJylgE0vtlisjqzrHk8yLubKSr49VxFEflKijbZar3JJoNWp_37IO4qX_Cmg==&c=ReX80-RbqVEJnRS3wv1c3kbzsNZFPqkOchebzTIo7rlxLr9t7Sci5Q==&ch=p0A8iygy_VOLzSSt1gRNdI3guXz7Iq_kHYtACpn_7BKYmy_OattiYQ==)and help build a stronger, bolder NEGOP.
Now, I'm excited to share that I'll be visiting Scottsbluff next week for the first of our statewide Roundtable Discussions, hosted by Government Relations Director Jeanne Reigle. This event will feature Senator Brian Hardin (LD48) and a special appearance by Congressman Adrian Smith, with other guests expected as well.
We'll discuss Healthcare Access and Affordability in Nebraska, followed by a Q&A:
A Venue on Broadway, 17 W 16th St, Scottsbluff Thursday, August 14 1:30 - 3:00 PM
Please RSVP to [govrelations@ne.gop](mailto:govrelations@ne.gop) as space is limited. Confirmed guests will be seated first. No large handbags, backpacks, or signs will be allowed.
Lastly, if you're interested in running for office, let's connect! The NEGOP is partnering with the RNC to host campaign training bootcamps in the coming weeks, and we're always looking for leaders ready to step up.
Thank you for your continued support. Let's keep Nebraska red!

Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party

Fellow activist,

Over the last decade, decisions coming out of Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court have positively impacted millions of Pennsylvanians:

They have granted environmental groups the right to intervene in cases affecting greenhouse gas regulations, declared that Pennsylvania’s 2011 U.S. congressional district map violated the Pennsylvania Constitution and required a new map to be drawn, ruled against several attempts directed to contest the votes of millions of Pennsylvanians, most notably from the 2020 election cycle, and more._**If MAGA is successful with their anti-retention campaign, the PA Supreme Court could have three vacancies until the Governor can appoint and the PA Senate approves their replacements (which is unlikely, as this would require a two-thirds majority approval from the Republican controlled PA Senate) or until the next judicial election in 2027. **_

**We are counting on you to help us ensure that voters know what is at stake. **
[**An independent and fair judiciary is needed now more than ever before in the Commonwealth. Will you help us fund a major Get-Out-The-Vote program to engage and mobilize Pennsylvanians for these key races by rushing $25 or more today?**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7cnf2nT45L4TDLL79xclFmlYUKVz-2FZIWg-2FtTcyX6kz-2Fldqn4tu3YHEopB7bFLjVBKDA14X2X02fgcUGQDy0selH0HoBjPfnr91B1eOGIl6Dh3H-2B4nqtreV-2BwStqcsoF1U-3D7ILa_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYZX4rxVGq13FAveBto8poiTfCDF2CfGCtaoDDFqt9BD-2Ba9K9oHOKKAy5UfHuYPVU4-2Fa0jEvLeP-2FbNNKyYnt1amsI9g1p9xtEO-2FvNmRcg2oIndBTGX7ZOIsZsiUFwmHkNXfD-2FILAD5FIyFQ-2Flw-2BN2cvhhUyqlEY1s3EJnmeLmU-2FOGDuT4G-2B-2F95wFUnaUnSqZsR)

\----------- Forwarded message ---------

Fellow activist,

We are only 3 months away from the November elections in PA, and to demonstrate just how critical the state Supreme Court retention elections are, we wanted to highlight some recent decisions made by the PA Supreme Court that impact our everyday lives.

**Take a look at a few examples:**
[****](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7N1bdan8dqslucv3braa57iroH1-2FNyT9glGiUy6uuKR-2F-2BsGqrbQ23aFzSDWcFvVwGKyx9QuJslIPj5jLKud7LKGG5-2BtNXJJLXPqatepk1Lb7h5QhUsC-2BSda2YEYBk9rs8-3DgqZD_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYZX4rxVGq13FAveBto8poiTfCDF2CfGCtaoDDFqt9BD9qNdKyoMhIz-2FDN60HCSgAPPjVD1EFmhfXMc2oy3lEGjUPI5ImpXbJOTzyQlb1A55dyhkJHTiXzP1qViXqnjih5ur7S6Bzeq-2F806bMdEBiUNoye64-2BdofrPHN-2FC-2F-2B4-2F7TeKsFoRw3F09ruuOgCiYAfw)
---
* **Education (William Penn SD v. PA Dept. of Education):** A landmark decision ruling that PA’s school funding system is unconstitutional, alleging that the state failed to adequately and equitably fund public education.
* **Gerrymandering (League of Women Voters v. Commonwealth):** Congressional maps in PA were struck down as unconstitutional partisan gerrymander under the state constitution, and the state Supreme Court ordered the General Assembly to submit a new redistricting plan.
* **Voting Rights (Genser v. Butler County Board of Elections):** After two voters had their provisional ballots rejected during the 2024 PA primary election, the PA Supreme Court ruled that voters whose mail ballots are rejected may cast valid provisional ballots.
* **Abortion Rights (Allegheny Reproductive Health Ctr v. DHS):** The PA Supreme Court ruled that the state statute that bans abortion coverage through the Medicaid program violated the Equal Rights Amendment and equal protection provisions of the PA state Constitution.
* **Environmental Protection (Shirley v. Legislative Reference Bureau):** It was ruled that nonprofits can intervene to defend environmental regulations under the ERA.
[****](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7N1bdan8dqslucv3braa57iroH1-2FNyT9glGiUy6uuKR-2F-2BsGqrbQ23aFzSDWcFvVwGKyx9QuJslIPj5jLKud7LKGG5-2BtNXJJLXPqatepk1Lb7h5QhUsC-2BSda2YEYBk9rs8-3DirAr_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYZX4rxVGq13FAveBto8poiTfCDF2CfGCtaoDDFqt9BD8wWYQYhT3tGqPS1KFF0DzexFO1nJcPaPTrzMBRyIFa1Am1MVDLT6nxL-2BFuurdmdZ-2FUHtKiqs5OheoyEfz7hHkbdE7YjgEJtsFngOv1i4Wm1TJf0d7uwrJJM-2B9CFrnSFx3g3R1kovz5WX9XBfsyc9FC)

**Here’s the deal:** Republicans are running an anti-retention campaign against three Democratically elected justices on the PA Supreme Court. They see these retention elections as their best chance to reverse the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court. If they are successful, we can expect to see very different judicial decisions.

Time is running out, and we need your help:
[**Please help support our voter education and mobilization work ahead of these highly consequential judicial retention elections by donating $25 or whatever you can now, and have twice the impact.
**](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7cnf2nT45L4TDLL79xclFmlYUKVz-2FZIWg-2FtTcyX6kz-2Fldqn4tu3YHEopB7bFLjVBKDA14X2X02fgcUGQDy0seld0bIYlVpvrqmBfUv9CB1dUOegX5kDBLzGEd4c4gKs8w-3DhJev_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYZX4rxVGq13FAveBto8poiTfCDF2CfGCtaoDDFqt9BD8kjogsEzEMO6d95gQiE2XYSVcM8CACfs-2FK8dYFD-2FzVWzQc3HM9BQbnlbdwZr1GXoHN57Ol2DpP1YY-2F8PDUBJhsK63ajVHTY6OexThJ-2BgLBAW39e4Ips9lGy4OoJwRESf4uGXmiomU0lk-2B74wLDqVxT)

_Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round.
_

| | _The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth._

clean_body: | | | Fellow activist, Over the last decade, decisions coming out of Pennsylvania’s Supreme Court have positively impacted millions of Pennsylvanians: They have granted environmental groups the right to intervene in cases affecting greenhouse gas regulations, declared that Pennsylvania’s 2011 U.S. congressional district map violated the Pennsylvania Constitution and required a new map to be drawn, ruled against several attempts directed to contest the votes of millions of Pennsylvanians, most notably from the 2020 election cycle, and more.If MAGA is successful with their anti-retention campaign, the PA Supreme Court could have three vacancies until the Governor can appoint and the PA Senate approves their replacements (which is unlikely, as this would require a two-thirds majority approval from the Republican controlled PA Senate) or until the next judicial election in 2027. We are counting on you to help us ensure that voters know what is at stake. Thank you, Katy PersonetteChief Development and Grants Officer \----------- Forwarded message --------- Fellow activist, We are only 3 months away from the November elections in PA, and to demonstrate just how critical the state Supreme Court retention elections are, we wanted to highlight some recent decisions made by the PA Supreme Court that impact our everyday lives. Take a look at a few examples: Education (William Penn SD v. PA Dept. of Education): A landmark decision ruling that PA’s school funding system is unconstitutional, alleging that the state failed to adequately and equitably fund public education. Gerrymandering (League of Women Voters v. Commonwealth): Congressional maps in PA were struck down as unconstitutional partisan gerrymander under the state constitution, and the state Supreme Court ordered the General Assembly to submit a new redistricting plan. Voting Rights (Genser v. Butler County Board of Elections): After two voters had their provisional ballots rejected during the 2024 PA primary election, the PA Supreme Court ruled that voters whose mail ballots are rejected may cast valid provisional ballots. Abortion Rights (Allegheny Reproductive Health Ctr v. DHS): The PA Supreme Court ruled that the state statute that bans abortion coverage through the Medicaid program violated the Equal Rights Amendment and equal protection provisions of the PA state Constitution. Environmental Protection (Shirley v. Legislative Reference Bureau): It was ruled that nonprofits can intervene to defend environmental regulations under the ERA. Here’s the deal: Republicans are running an anti-retention campaign against three Democratically elected justices on the PA Supreme Court. They see these retention elections as their best chance to reverse the Democratic majority on the PA Supreme Court. If they are successful, we can expect to see very different judicial decisions. Time is running out, and we need your help: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Thank you, New Pennsylvania Project Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round. | | The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth. Will you support us with a monthly contribution today? || || || The New Pennsylvania Project is a 501(c)(4). Contributions are not for charitable purposes and are not tax deductible. | Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project Our Contact Information** New Pennsylvania ProjectP.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381412-699-6771 | | | |

subject: Morning folks! Have time for a short message?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
I got up early today, poured myself a nice big cup of coffee, and now I’m sitting outside enjoying some fresh air and catalpa trees before the busy sets in.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113013575/562361407/1435897075?refcode=email_080925_morning-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNDUxYjcyOC0yMjc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6rFPH47r5hZqXQiyb_dRPIjcKmtwHq98P5rIXJq02_I=&emci=e39e1eb7-f272-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=b451b728-2275-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=7280715) Morning, folks! I got up early today, poured myself a nice big cup of coffee, and now I’m sitting outside enjoying some fresh air and catalpa trees before the busy sets in. Before the day starts, I’m taking a look at some of our fundraising numbers and we’re a bit behind where I’d like us to be at this point in the month. Summer is usually a tough season for fundraising, and with the GOP gearing up to expand their majority by flipping this seat red, we can’t afford to fall behind now. **[So, I’m personally asking if you’ll chip in $10 or anything you can to help us keep Colorado blue? name: Mark Kelly for Senate
subject: PLEASE: Don’t click away just yet 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Team:
Don't click away just yet.
**FIRST:**
Mark won some of the most competitive elections in the country in 2020 and 2022 without taking a cent from corporate PACs. Millions of people donated. We’re proud of that fact, and you should be, too.

**THEN:**
Trump and our opponents absolutely pummeled Mark through both of those campaigns. He had hundreds of millions of dollars spent against him in 2020 and 2022, and Trump personally attacked Mark.

**NOW:**
Mark is at the same place he’s been his entire life. On the frontline, defending our democracy, serving where he can make the most difference. And Trump’s allies are going after him for it, which means he needs our help.
The attacks Mark receives every day from the MAGA Right and Trump’s top operatives are serious.
But none of that is going to stop Mark from defending our democracy and taking the fight to Trump when it really matters. If you understand how important it is that somebody like Mark is working to turn the tide against Trump, we’re humbly asking you:

This is my hail Mary...
| [](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/k-nZHJ-VdDtr4k6BeOpL-HiPHCSu_ezsAYC_ngaJP6GY8UM1wI7XeM7MOStxFTb9kRKjEE4DYkJVWF9MsCepXobGZaRU95f4OeTcDlGfKOFZfWaDlDyySOskIi18bAQNECE9iDcTHpnmXVFV4ROlk6cplndzucZFMSz6oC1jV-9_prMnRy3wwq62MOY5FbR4Tf2uPYiHgckQuqJUPvy2I2HYqT5OkXdMrj8PTGTs2fKj4iv6iSQU3UBVls6WTlV9SXxztDSSawFIffqmng059-WUE8x6yuVCHVLjKNqybNJ9urWAhK4FFFrRYAOxqpfcDsSsqnCFY1Fx27Wn0trfuMVGfA9gjrIdZuWBB2O-bEM1eCo2o6lst-7L1QPjhtP0TESY-xuql7FbxGuubMwbOceIX5cZugvH8vYPXAVWBeL-A76jd-yk2hkEzdQDfU0LNwa3BgVKeWgxLaSeNjjuM3wbVerDwFvECTxyKJKWSDRAmBtytPXWTj7JGWLG03dzeC9cakbpi0zz55ov7gxVlRkec23Z6ZXT7i1GyXCnIh96OyUgJjJFJnjUtH0TNh4SDIyWCpk59AQ_zEuuVXE4AnI0EwbBV6lMREO1HBGeWQmu-przLL-y-kPgErVd9jqO4NiHIHzz1hVqh9YVB-UOOSkzeBCoD-bZwKcSW51EXbY35WZChuS4C7KM0RHC6qJlG6BtOZd_y0rmr-kru-oK5IBJ5oNqfKeZQiYnthoGEQGRamLBjjobtkyh9r6GMlEewEjjFt__Pi4IElgNQLOBMi2GNj-mAvs0IG6qmjvbOYSZkx2nyRlldfjSPv7wCOQwebzvpabI-LrbmDePKyayWKwCZykVEumEcl7rMQPgg-IB1R9q0w/3NXWihNTZtmugaEpg6bdefEmINXdbTjo/11)

Dear Peter,
---
This is the most personal letter I’ve ever written.
---
I’m writing to you not as America’s Mayor, not as a former prosecutor, not even as President Trump’s former attorney, but as an 80-year-old man – a grandfather and a patriot – staring down a 20-year prison sentence for doing what I believed was right.
---
I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all.
---
_Or so I thought..._
---
**...But now, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
They think they’ve finally got me! Left me with no escape, no lifeline – especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!
---
_No pardon.
No protection.
No cavalry coming over the hill. _
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful _force_ absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me: **_the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like _YOU_._**
---
My mugshot was entered under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — [**“THE PEOPLE’S PARDON”**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/EqC7iTXTNi9ZbecRIoseQ6yZ7bceIxq2LhBebPWhenTQWfIWla90QBtOKjswxvCxErUIDWLBhmBCyprwQa0ejx881FUeGB3s80wJFoePzrKUUdXAsDcqvBYMz2R4--cmdwX3PO9nSx74wdQHg9nhG5S6g9L6Y_IYrcyTd2XWuqYnDAOnQDvyrOCC_nNF8gtnX0ta4Y93RQ7iqdhlecoQzeeWj-5EYwEbpi90tMWD4dt6bX4MdSnSKmN4_HnqbbEAJWWsR0LZv3jRwP_IePC4-iSMMc_pTS_gGnUUG8z1VZ7sE073AecfNAlxyeXPx0EKWQLDP6_FFLPGIOUo3iXlHZfTgBiiEvBCLg78fL747C142fV_0Iniy_lD-vAZ7TgrIJq93PWfVjlxXzcvwo23D39nYIEWRhT9eVa6jgS3wHfzyTHldKNHO8huGbPzovF1CcHpDkLf0Q8aodpqjvCQWnSJOBeWaw1fyRAAi2wUC2fq8PrYaVUl2Z72U1cVbNP9Zj9QyF_5GVQ1_DHmgVAXR-SwrG5Kdmnqpst7gg_vC-etTC42DodWuaYvMvtVcKrs5hRJPk3ibJQ1yR32OHrXOdCINh8m-omLTueq1VVRVCOOQx_32fsxDQOFL4yQ5uXbuiZ4H03PBbMaxwbr1x6KDKLPsvTvFMYGsjwou-i4DFndiE6hMC4MriUlqDpY2ttDswlMQasS6skQGwhmUGULGIRlyywbKzGi9RIOSmPbpTz5WsavnDaQ6JV24nk8aU23iedgLJ4Q2SzAKKy1_YDWsHICuFp4HRkEdBOLxonSCBXXzlnjaMiDG-rteMqVhVyn1siU81F6xLr-6_hZCPbLQmjdUgbhGQVXGS2HC3lhv2LCN_rBew/tLM_Jtd3Ty6CylgubTbxrHRDAg7l8efR/11) — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES.
---
[**Please help me reach this life-saving (_truly!_) goal today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/Dkk4qmV1hZF3f0fTDzA0YtE2XS020-HmIZ3EdguK8xJZ-ZLxMgvMUyqCkbDxSTIWaSxcmSq3VuPb_fWiMhS0oWH_g24v_a_EMskJFe3XjDlvpykfWf1mXFfNxtzzeMPsWbg_jYkUxTfoOyfa0b84YwQAmW8H1AheotQyW49tueMXrKml956G2VRtrYAr4wI1rXI5cwxm14JlKK4mccI4SIiLzk5XV4GeMOwc5MwsBK0VAxQ5WzHwkgElrFffLBOLXa3C65Pi5CbN_6xdz3ZjhKdzt1YWPy1ZXjS_dlaWzKRHI-aKc7PTl6d88Q8TMFmPACxCz37NEOsVk4afXGikLmvUwAR0UaBP07xo5lH_naTSARcf4LmkqXFhg22VsWhS4jxcfHanP7bu2vBi3InkFv8-oKWWA4Oxy5n2opAr_bBkqmrYzydHi4SltZov5HbpgVODVkrgx6D-a0JYwqXROJmsfd7Jt6NZhUSIpixo5vNgnGe7vVJEiZBNBb8P99_pak5SSpL55tO6JPmGvPXcft0EVEU9iRlGMpqKCowIh5KZ-ewWp4uwjZ0iH3l0ymI0yllloz8jUHd-qrGh8EJNcT7ga_qaZCH_72ZB1CvPzSWA1tmZQgSt8UKkcw5JkdpWbkR_Gv17tr6tYdYFDvmGKUs9v6MjrPEqCTSjcCEWhaBaGKdHI_AlWipgGHqw422Litz0nHfQEpvIq7UKN3D_HxeSTApgmWF0ch4s8_3VkgU69bNCDsR_uUp9-HQ6aQPLX-y-CeYYKOWh8UYvEYC-nH8pgPFY3Hx8lSQNJye0kpyEaKAG4bnzXHmgwjHBOoAWeXGcNgD2tgNMKm7je3PwUi-LvRbCEGeWdPkQ95B5ebXM-k3rYQ/GTZ_r1MJuy_vneOYmtByiv2uKpFV-PuZ/11)

clean_body: This is my hail Mary... | Dear Peter, This is the most personal letter I’ve ever written. I’m writing to you not as America’s Mayor, not as a former prosecutor, not even as President Trump’s former attorney, but as an 80-year-old man – a grandfather and a patriot – staring down a 20-year prison sentence for doing what I believed was right. I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all. Or so I thought... ...But now, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a federal pardon in state court. They think they’ve finally got me! Left me with no escape, no lifeline – especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses! No pardon. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill. But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful force absolutely can: THE AMERICAN PEOPLE. So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me: the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU. My mugshot was entered under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES. Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust |

Hi Peter,

We are proud to announce our endorsement of Mira Tanna for Orlando City Council in Florida.

**For almost 15 years, Mira has been helping her community thrive through local government and local nonprofits. While her opponents have limited experience with city government, she has achieved countless real results, including securing $80M in funding for the people of Orlando.**

[**One of Mira’s main opponents, Jason Albu, has a history of donating to Ron DeSantis and a tough, conservative track record to go along with it. Florida needs a stronger defense against DeSantis, and electing better leaders across the state is the best place to start. At Her Bold Move, we always ask our candidates the following question: If elected, describe how you will support women and issues that are important to the women in your district/city/state.

Here’s how Mira responded to that question – and why we are excited to see what she will do in office:

_**Expanding the Social Safety Net -** I would like to expand the Orlando Kidz Zones, which provide services to lift families out of poverty. It provides access to early childhood programs and parenting support to get kids off to the right start, and helps support high graduation rates and career opportunities for the city’s youth.

**Improving School Safety and Mental Health Resources -** I would continue to expand mentoring access to girls in order to improve the school climate and reduce disciplinary incidents in schools. I would like to expand the Community Response Team that provides trained behavioral health providers when 911 callers indicate that there is a mental health emergency. Currently, the city can only respond to 1600 of the 20K annual mental health calls with a trained behavioral health team.

**Women’s & Babies Hygiene -** I would like to tackle period poverty and diaper needs by providing free reusable menstrual products and cloth diapers to low-income women and girls. I believe this strategy could pay for itself by decreasing landfill costs, decreasing costly sewage blockages, and decreasing contamination of recycling streams. _

**Mira believes people should feel a sense of connection to their local government. She is eager to engage residents _most impacted_ by local government decisions, including the voices who are unlikely to be heard. **

[**Importantly, what sets Mira apart from her opponents is her energized people-powered campaign on the ground, and wealth of experience in the city council. You can be a part of that effort by splitting a $20.25 or more donation now.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hbmfl-tanna/?refcode=EM_F_250808Tanna_N_CD_90DO_10_NG_N_L2&amount=20.25)

We hope you’ll join us,

Colleen Davis
Endorsements Manager
Her Bold Move
---

name: Shontel Brown
subject: Local grocery stores could close.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
For every $5 in new SNAP benefits, up to $9 of economic activity is generated.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113016929/562372791/1806897946?refcode=EM_F_250808Stores_SB_90DO_6_NG_B_P1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YzE4ZDBmNi0yNDc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1VPg2IBIrJ2bOUkHHjCVGaYB4lfTHgVOEwl7rSXRp28=) Peter, this is exactly what I was afraid of: It was recently reported that Trump’s Big Ugly Bill could cause local grocery stores to _close_.

Trump and his GOP cronies in the House worked together to slash $186 billion from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) through 2034, cutting the program's funding by around 20%. These changes could cause over half of the 40 million people who depend on SNAP to lose some or ALL of their benefits.

According to _CNN_ , cutting food assistance benefits recklessly could also cause 27,000 retailers nationwide to close because most of their customers rely on SNAP benefits for groceries.

**For every $5 in new SNAP benefits, up to $9 of economic activity is generated. This is just another example of Republicans actively working to damage our economy and harm our communities to fund their next ballroom renovation.**

It is unacceptable. Many of these grocery stores that will close will be in low-income areas with large shares of SNAP recipients and regions that are already food deserts. We must hold Republicans accountable for their destruction.

body:

Peter, we have fewer than 100 days to go until Election Day!
I'm the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue, and we're in the homestretch now. **[Before I lay out all we're doing to win the biggest race of 2025, will you consider splitting a contribution between my campaign and State Democrats Committee to power our work?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93171?refcode=em20250809-9247&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9247%2E614464%2EBSSsRX)**

Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face.
_And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources._
My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." _We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here._
I've given voters my word: **As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington.**
We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward.
That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills.
With the days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to:
✅ Visit every corner of Virginia.
✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks.
✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen.
It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what the National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond.
**[Everything depends on us raising what we need from grassroots donors. So, I'm asking: will you pitch in $10 or more to my campaign and State Democrats Committee to help us win on November 4th and in the years ahead?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93171?refcode=em20250809-9247&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=9247%2E614464%2EBSSsRX)**

clean_body: We have to make each day left in this campaign count. Peter, we have fewer than 100 days to go until Election Day! I'm the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue, and we're in the homestretch now. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face. And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources. My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here. I've given voters my word: As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington. We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward. That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills. With the days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to: ✅ Visit every corner of Virginia. ✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks. ✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen. It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what the National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: I'm grateful to have you on my team, Abigail Spanberger Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

subject: They’re hiding the damage they’ve done
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ISAIAH MARTIN FOR CONGRESS
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109386/images/IRM_wrapper-email.jpg]
friend, hey — it’s Isaiah Martin's Team.
Please take a look at the newest ways Trump and his cronies are targeting the environment.
They’re Deleting the EvidenceURGENT • ENVIRONMENTThey’re deleting the evidence.
A new report shows Trump’s second-term agencies have made 70% more changes to federal environmental websites than his first term — pulling down tools, burying climate pages, and erasing environmental justice resources that communities rely on to defend their health and their rights.
What’s happening
* Climate & EJ pages removed or altered: key tools like the Climate & Economic Justice Screening Tool were taken down; climate.gov stopped publishing after the team was terminated.
* Public oversight weakened: agencies are “refocusing” by scrubbing DEI and environmental justice — the very info that helps communities fight pollution.
* Democracy impact: when the government hides data, the public loses the power to hold polluters (and politicians) accountable.
Websites are how the government talks to the public. If they can erase the data, they can erase the damage.
Help stop the scrub.
This campaign is fighting for transparency, science, and communities on the frontlines. If they can erase the facts, they can justify anything. Let’s make sure they can’t.
Chip in to Fight Back [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin]Your donation helps organize, litigate, and hold the line while they try to hide the ball.
We deserve a government that publishes the truth — not one that deletes it. Thanks for having our back.
If you can’t give today, forward this email to three friends. Sunlight is still the best disinfectant.

Public oversight weakened: agencies are “refocusing” by scrubbing DEI and environmental justice — the very info that helps communities fight pollution. * Democracy impact: when the government hides data, the public loses the power to hold polluters (and politicians) accountable. body:
These are tactics straight out of the Jim Crow playbook, and they must be stopped.
|
---

Peter,
Gerrymandering hurts communities of color, diluting the power of their vote and ensuring their interests aren’t represented in the halls of power.
**It couldn’t be more blatantly unfair. People of color made up 95 percent of Texas’s population growth, with Texas’s Hispanic population now nearly as large as its white population. Texas also has three of the five fastest-growing cities in the country. **
But look at the congressional map state lawmakers just proposed — diluting the power of people of color and “cracking and packing” urban areas. They claim to be adding a new majority Latino seat, but they’re consolidating the number of seats where Latino communities can elect a candidate of their choice.
**All On The Line is mobilizing our grassroots supporters and using every tool at our disposal to fight back and raise the alarm about what’s happening in Texas.**
[**In this critical moment, will you step up with a donation of $15 so we can expose these attacks for what they are — shameless power grabs?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113020923/562384532/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_CallOutTX_email_20250809-AOTL-Tier1-CallOutTX_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250809-AOTL-Tier1-CallOutTX_&utm_campaign=CallOutTX&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVjOWYzNDI5LTI5NzUtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YXJ_p-IM0uq81u6YFXyf9R_4z2z_CMZ7EzHrgmBLXlo=)

Under the Voting Rights Act, states have an obligation to ensure communities of color are accurately represented by not diluting their votes when drawing up district maps. Let’s be clear: Texas is already falling short on this front — voters have spent years in court challenging the current congressional map. And when Trump demanded a “very simple redraw” that would enable Republicans to pick up five seats, he’s asking lawmakers to go even farther to dilute the voting power of Black and Hispanic voters.
**Make no mistake: What’s happening in Texas echoes some of the same despicable tactics that have been used since Reconstruction to suppress the vote. **
As Attorney General Eric Holder put it, “Our nation is experiencing the greatest attack on the right to vote since Jim Crow. These forces are not disguising their intent; in fact, they are reviving the very same arguments put forward by those who opposed the Civil Rights Movement.”
It isn’t hyperbole to say that this is a watershed moment for the future of American democracy. Each of us has a role to play in this fight to expose these racist tactics and protect every American’s access to the ballot.
[**If you’re with us, we need your help today — this fight is playing out as you read this. Make a contribution to All On The Line as we mobilize our grassroots movement to push back against these attempts to disenfranchise voters.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113020953/562384563/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_CallOutTX_email_20250809-AOTL-Tier1-CallOutTX_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250809-AOTL-Tier1-CallOutTX_&utm_campaign=CallOutTX&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVjOWYzNDI5LTI5NzUtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=YXJ_p-IM0uq81u6YFXyf9R_4z2z_CMZ7EzHrgmBLXlo=)

clean_body: These are tactics straight out of the Jim Crow playbook, and they must be stopped. | Peter, Gerrymandering hurts communities of color, diluting the power of their vote and ensuring their interests aren’t represented in the halls of power. It couldn’t be more blatantly unfair. People of color made up 95 percent of Texas’s population growth, with Texas’s Hispanic population now nearly as large as its white population. Texas also has three of the five fastest-growing cities in the country. But look at the congressional map state lawmakers just proposed — diluting the power of people of color and “cracking and packing” urban areas. They claim to be adding a new majority Latino seat, but they’re consolidating the number of seats where Latino communities can elect a candidate of their choice. All On The Line is mobilizing our grassroots supporters and using every tool at our disposal to fight back and raise the alarm about what’s happening in Texas. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Under the Voting Rights Act, states have an obligation to ensure communities of color are accurately represented by not diluting their votes when drawing up district maps. Let’s be clear: Texas is already falling short on this front — voters have spent years in court challenging the current congressional map. And when Trump demanded a “very simple redraw” that would enable Republicans to pick up five seats, he’s asking lawmakers to go even farther to dilute the voting power of Black and Hispanic voters. Make no mistake: What’s happening in Texas echoes some of the same despicable tactics that have been used since Reconstruction to suppress the vote. As Attorney General Eric Holder put it, “Our nation is experiencing the greatest attack on the right to vote since Jim Crow. These forces are not disguising their intent; in fact, they are reviving the very same arguments put forward by those who opposed the Civil Rights Movement.” It isn’t hyperbole to say that this is a watershed moment for the future of American democracy. Each of us has a role to play in this fight to expose these racist tactics and protect every American’s access to the ballot. = — Team AOTL is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. and | = | PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND. name: Chris Pappas
subject: Hate isn't welcome here
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
Let’s stand against hate and defend our shared values.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=1769%2E582294%2EhHuYQo)
A few days ago, a neo-Nazi group marched through Concord, waving swastikas and spreading vile hate speech right on our State House lawn.
**There is no place for any form of hate speech in New Hampshire.**
This is about more than one group or one day. It’s about who we are and who we refuse to become. We need leaders who stand up to hate speech — not echo it, excuse it, or ignore it.
Our campaign is about rejecting hate in all its forms — and building a future where everyone is treated with dignity, respect, and fairness.
We’ll keep organizing, showing up, and sending the message: _Hate isn’t welcome here._
[**Will you chip in $5 now to stand against hate and defend our shared values?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250809_1_hatespeech?refcode=em20250809-1769&amount=5&refcode2=1769_582294_hHuYQo&akid=1769%2E582294%2EhHuYQo)

With gratitude,
Chris Pappas
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**

---|---

name: Sal McBride
subject: It’s Sarah’s 35th birthday!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
I have to admit, I’m feeling extra sentimental today.
#
Hi! It’s Sal, Sarah’s mom.
I have to admit, I’m feeling extra sentimental today.
This is Sarah’s first birthday as _Delaware’s Congresswoman_ — and while she’s not one to make a fuss about gifts or attention, I didn’t want to let this day go by without doing something special.
**Because the truth is, to Sarah, public service isn’t about politics.** It’s about people. And in our small state of neighbors, she carries that responsibility into the halls of Congress with her single day.
**So today, I’d love your help in giving her a birthday gift she’ll truly appreciate: the resources to continue serving Delaware.**
[**Will you chip in $35 — or whatever you can — to celebrate Sarah’s 35th birthday and keep her voice in Congress?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250809-1?refcode=em20250809-2253&amount=35&refcode2=2253_21716_XFm-Tb&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)
When Sarah was little, she always had a strong sense of fairness.
That same spirit is what drives her every single day. She knows families are struggling with the cost of health care, child care, and housing — and she’s fighting like hell to not only defend Delawareans but also bring home real solutions.
As her mom, I couldn’t be more proud. As a Delawarean, I couldn’t be more grateful.
**Today, I hope she gets to feel just a little bit of love in return, from all of us who believe in the progress she’s fighting for.**
[**So, please pitch in $35 or anything you can spare in honor of Sarah’s birthday.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250809-1?refcode=em20250809-2253&amount=35&refcode2=2253_21716_XFm-Tb&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)
**[Donate $35 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-20250809-1?amount=35&refcode=em20250809-2253&refcode2=2253_21716_XFm-Tb&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)**
Thanks for supporting Sarah and having her back. It means so much to me as her mom to know she's not doing any of this alone.
Sal McBride
(Proud Mom of Delaware’s Congresswoman)
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250809-1&t=1&refcode2=2253_21716_XFm-Tb&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=2&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=3&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=2253%2E21716%2EXFm-Tb)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)

body:
When I first stepped into the political arena to run for U.S. Senate, I was told I didn’t stand a chance.
[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=3b136b23a94b447c87dff2c3094cca72&_e=cv8q8SYLiGOnBw9c09Zy-oJt1tUn0fksRViVkUodDtr-uM9v2E6UgTNten2118P6LGchDJK7kIzNcPd5hF4_i-fU5RmM-uRQPTVQMHleHtXSCyO6HLkxfaPQnOokVZJZEomlr5Pf9iZyP2zATZeCSbedRmDlkcLz68wEYOqftocQw0haQ_ewoUnko61-0e_NCqL_UmU5jr0rnv6B5lLzNmSWhMCkis1lFyFKp4U1PWmIo7iY4-M-IH3t0y3K9ORM7wnHBDVGX2tuP1AxeYND-IJ9cj8go7cD6o-aO5hwunMEDSq_1TWvoqTkd_AzMI0pbvwsil1WPiY93Bi7Cx9jzNCoBau5lIUVnd8VO9h71-Vy41gyPPX8HuEhhQCNW6KKmG5ALVxmkhUrlFSqTIjhm4FlBP30QC6YnElFmTMc8B2LNyruwpHnkmm-_taSaleHJ95Zngm4sv-p3JWVAUq2rrMNwlnIkXbj4SDs-pbGn-1zQ2CL5PWemL-_HKr401KGtqXULRqbyQAgtrMWMk45CNzZsP3vQ_2gXMVoO3pFrmyH3VC2UUg8U-NuldVnvOy6H8QOTbCmtCHN8C3P-G3R2pzTkWG7tMMRptj07KdWkZ75_kZom9isrJQVbzCVr0U64oTRYxDFk2kChxUGLIy6FhI5a6xSAlmXhk4xYTjYzmQgUIZsS-1OE8g_JF6SuJ_vBKf5ZfQ9clh1Q5frET2HgORS8ADOFj7UYHTIoWAEKERZpgFGsE9fnwtSR7oIGMWD)When I first stepped into the political arena to run for U.S. Senate, I was told I didn't stand a chance.
The talking heads of legacy media mocked me.
The Democrat Machine dumped $55 million into Ohio to crush my campaign.
They outspent us 3-to-1. Every liberal group in America piled on.
They didn't just want to beat me. They wanted to humiliate me. To send a message: outsiders like me aren't welcome.
But I had one thing they didn't: YOU.
We clawed our way through, dollar by dollar, grassroots supporter by grassroots supporter - and we won. Not by a little. By over six points!
Now, I'm watching that same story unfold again - this time with my friend Vivek Ramaswamy in his race for Governor of Ohio.
And just like in my race, the Democrat Machine is already unloading millions, launching smears, and pulling out every dirty trick in the book to end his campaign.
[That’s why I’m asking – respectfully – for you to help Vivek like you helped me:](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=3b136b23a94b447c87dff2c3094cca72&_e=cv8q8SYLiGOnBw9c09Zy-oJt1tUn0fksRViVkUodDtr-uM9v2E6UgTNten2118P6LGchDJK7kIzNcPd5hF4_i-fU5RmM-uRQPTVQMHleHtXSCyO6HLkxfaPQnOokVZJZEomlr5Pf9iZyP2zATZeCSbedRmDlkcLz68wEYOqftocQw0haQ_ewoUnko61-0e_NCqL_UmU5jr0rnv6B5lLzNmSWhMCkis1lFyFKp4U1PWmIo7iY4-M-IH3t0y3K9ORM7wnHBDVGX2tuP1AxeYND-HJ9Q2ALBk12OisBpL4A9lx5AZPfZjO5gehNJpZFgmvGR0GnWrRNfqImlY0Nb63o1sAK_Rg7Ev1hI11aEwiTEAQbKPp4I-nvN2dMvDzpHsCkkNLkfeORhHz9KOiJhjM_af68dFVYpi7FDsXJY56jMyI2qFhYrSRQ2OcrdtAQbnjfAqN2BtFzTxsxTSgWKjGvZo0aqlDu5c42shTMNcQDqNsLYVya1KQH9KFSPg4m8W6Ppya_B1UXydQA_dAvnEd9hl_LaEm_VCjlMEP9zC_KjilCuBVJlJvl5hExigF5jxQPrX9Pc0baOcDX48rixH41umhsWK3TrfTRNyXLOwnVSbgeBx0a3uaEFgMCKLZXjjJWFVBOHQXx28wAdJukgUI1dDpSpnN5nlF02Kp4RzAQqP4HgE-1sfQxtBI1Xo0iUByfb3dREMYhK6Mz-T2cttJt_HGvnOL4oCygzOMGAAZN7EaFf1vngqWO3eunIKEfpwER)[CHIP IN A FEW BUCKS

He's a problem for The Establishment and bureaucratic class.**
**He 'll gut the Deep State -- from Columbus to Washington.He'll deport every illegal alien - enforce law and order.He'll roll back transgenderism - ban men in women's sports, no sex changes for minors. He'll terminate all DEI programs - root out woke propaganda. He'll overhaul failing schools - expand school choice and reward excellent teachers.He'll obliterate the managerial bureaucracy - take a hacksaw to government bloat. He'll restore national identity - promote merit and patriotism, and reject victimhood.**
**President Trump and I are working every day to deliver the America First agenda you voted for. But we can 't do it alone. We need MAGA leaders in every state - especially battlegrounds like Ohio.
[So here’s my ask: Will you chip in $10, $25, or whatever you can to help Vivek win this race?](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=3b136b23a94b447c87dff2c3094cca72&_e=cv8q8SYLiGOnBw9c09Zy-oJt1tUn0fksRViVkUodDtr-uM9v2E6UgTNten2118P6LGchDJK7kIzNcPd5hF4_i-fU5RmM-uRQPTVQMHleHtXSCyO6HLkxfaPQnOokVZJZEomlr5Pf9iZyP2zATZeCSbedRmDlkcLz68wEYOqftocQw0haQ_ewoUnko61-0e_NCqL_UmU5jr0rnv6B5lLzNmSWhMCkis1lFyFKp4U1PWmIo7iY4-M-IH3t0y3K9ORM7wnHBDVGX2tuP1AxeYND-M6AQNJsIV8zGg5G0iwdrLnE6WeGi8GQdLtF7zpfvmLIxjLGcvaksNo3Z1GUY5dgTWoQ0OQ8oAaZNNxdHU_lRsibs9gIHsX1GsPtb9LTPEzqyCDQqL4LH39Pl0_A36yXg80EFemeoqMdC-Ae0QBw13WPD-0bZ2VuK_ovynIdFhbRE3joLuq4eHFVEPM-ytqOaY-5yTOxM4PY5Y947iRW3ZztUj-77PC_Ku7STYxWATK6WBdOYqLTFdALgevyEpKKyIgVQZbSonomvQhC2kZ17MZSYn2FeYLU6sKVhfQ5wgzI5WKsC-eFYWckLexygORFtY5rBYz4X83trRKIK3EkLF9uWS4qqB5taIRrlD_Pki3IYqSXCoipBK6gdeefjBjklw0hatk_ym9h_eykT2dABBWaVbXJVPIycCZjk9F9kgUDGUh9zuy6tl28VCD66RN1Si_MR4XEmFRyVM4I6kbBAe_Qb-Cp70K8a9Ww1oknw5vj)[CHIP IN A FEW BUCKS

**But it won 't happen unless MAGA patriots from around the country step up and help him. **
**I hope I can count on you to be one of them.
God bless you and God Bless America, [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=3b136b23a94b447c87dff2c3094cca72&_e=cv8q8SYLiGOnBw9c09Zy-oJt1tUn0fksRViVkUodDtr-uM9v2E6UgTNten2118P6LGchDJK7kIzNcPd5hF4_i-fU5RmM-uRQPTVQMHleHtXSCyO6HLkxfaPQnOokVZJZEomlr5Pf9iZyP2zATZeCSbedRmDlkcLz68wEYOqftocQw0haQ_ewoUnko61-0e_NCqL_UmU5jr0rnv6B5lLzNmSWhMCkis1lFyFKp4U1PWmIo7iY4-M-IH3t0y3K9ORM7wnHBDVGX2tuP1AxeYND-HKZ3iQojvbYFV5ek-j8Pexw7uaOd0ZOpDnRN9Z72er6haFp5Xc-CaHIKNesOixflap7dHihw3h8HKoiBDLqwgdxmGR_hRTuuH0yNLIeZl1Wct0hDS9klFuEC2lYaEdlpZE7dWAMybuD-JpEyQloY12FtWVMToU4h4GH-lgwOnMGgs_OLRRmnyyomudFl7-KedGo2pJRrihAhex-rGoESBuZFkuEeZhsgPLa400wXxtwDNNPI6UWBWNFeAJqrUvv9s8sko9gUXW_ZaeQ6mfzf5FnhSSxYoRNB7ln7Ewvp3eHxXi8Qsbi86sgkv1RWKk_I1ZwhfyMgku4_yeyfPEjy8q1xOhVRDeqUt8pUq3QmJdWjLpKGETxSjipaeA53F1pgZl_5vJF9mCPA92cqSnXwgOnykwbOmwIZFeGtkXfa2ngUbM3OQ4JWJIVCF8gb4YbMn9EciTrVqcWix1LtY2lEkBjYH9vQFzu-dd4HoihkDuu)JD VanceVice President. clean_body: When I first stepped into the political arena to run for U.S. Senate, I was told I didn’t stand a chance. I first stepped into the political arena to run for U.S. Senate, I was told I didn't stand a chance. The talking heads of legacy media mocked me. The Democrat Machine dumped $55 million into Ohio to crush my campaign. They outspent us 3-to-1. Every liberal group in America piled on. They didn't just want to beat me. They wanted to humiliate me. To send a message: outsiders like me aren't welcome. But I had one thing they didn't: YOU. We clawed our way through, dollar by dollar, grassroots supporter by grassroots supporter - and we won. Not by a little. By over six points! Now, I'm watching that same story unfold again - this time with my friend Vivek Ramaswamy in his race for Governor of Ohio. And just like in my race, the Democrat Machine is already unloading millions, launching smears, and pulling out every dirty trick in the book to end his campaign. not a politician. He's a problem for The Establishment and bureaucratic class. He 'll gut the Deep State -- from Columbus to Washington.He'll deport every illegal alien - enforce law and order.He'll roll back transgenderism - ban men in women's sports, no sex changes for minors. He'll terminate all DEI programs - root out woke propaganda. He'll overhaul failing schools - expand school choice and reward excellent teachers.He'll obliterate the managerial bureaucracy - take a hacksaw to government bloat. He'll restore national identity - promote merit and patriotism, and reject victimhood. President Trump and I are working every day to deliver the America First agenda you voted for. But we can 't do it alone. We need MAGA leaders in every state - especially battlegrounds like Ohio. been in this exact fight. I know what it takes to win, and I know Vivek has what it takes to lead. But it won 't happen unless MAGA patriots from around the country step up and help him. I hope I can count on you to be one of them. God bless you and God Bless America, VanceVice President. Proud Ohioan. is Vivek. I'm building a movement to make Ohio the state of American excellence: Reviving our schools. Reclaiming our values. Restoring pride in our country. But I can't do it alone. I need your voice behind me. | | | | | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO 350 E 1st Ave Ste 100 Columbus, OH 43201

name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Help support safe streets in Minneapolis
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
We deserve safe streets and thriving communities where people can easily get around
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1753%2E791427%2Emwj65Z)

[](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)
Dear friend,
We deserve safe streets and thriving communities where people can easily get around—not just by car, but by walking, biking, rolling, and transit. I hope you’ll join me in supporting a bold, community-led vision in Near North Minneapolis that prioritizes reparative justice and racial equity.
**[If that’s all you need to hear, please make a $10 contribution to Our Streets as they work on an amazing project to create safe streets for all. Otherwise, please continue reading!**](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)
**[Support the Vision!](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)**
Right now, Our Streets—a local transportation justice organization—is working with Near North residents to bring back 6th Avenue North and reclaim highway land that has polluted and divided the neighborhood for generations.
Once called the “Beale Street of Minneapolis,” this area was a thriving Black and Jewish community until it was destroyed in 1939 to make way for Olson Highway—a highway to nowhere, built through the heart of a vibrant neighborhood.
Olson Highway sees less traffic than narrower roadways like Broadway Avenue. Yet its intersections are the most dangerous in Minneapolis.
That’s why Our Streets is leading the **Bring Back 6th** campaign: to remove the highway, restore the historic street grid, reconnect communities, and build a safer, more equitable future. And they have the unanimous support of the Minneapolis City Council.
[](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)
But here’s the challenge: **The $1.6 million federal grant for this work is now at risk.**
Due to Trump’s executive orders, the agency overseeing their grant has been gutted. Now, more than $2 billion in Reconnecting Communities and Neighborhood Access and Equity grants are at risk, including two right here in Minnesota. This uncertainty puts serious financial strain on Our Streets and threatens delays — or even stoppage — to this historic work. These potential cuts, currently being debated in the federal budget reconciliation bill, would derail critical efforts across the country to repair past transportation harms and reconnect communities.
This devastating decision cuts off future opportunities for communities like Near North to access federal support for reparative infrastructure projects, making this work more urgent than ever.
Bringing back 6th could provide immense economic benefits, housing opportunities, reconnection and safer streets, alternative transportation options, and improved public health.
**[So please: Make a donation of $10 to help ensure that Our Streets’ Bring Back 6th efforts and the community’s vision don’t get stalled by bureaucratic chaos. Your support allows us to keep building momentum through public events, community outreach, and continued pressure on decision-makers.**](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)
**[Donate here today!](https://secure.engageraise.com/donate/ilhan-omar-and-our-streets/?refcode=io_em250809-1-180)**
After decades of disinvestment, Near North Minneapolis deserves a street that reflects the needs and dreams of its community—not outdated, dangerous infrastructure. Let’s Bring Back 6th. Let’s get this done.

clean_body: We deserve safe streets and thriving communities where people can easily get around Dear friend, We deserve safe streets and thriving communities where people can easily get around—not just by car, but by walking, biking, rolling, and transit. I hope you’ll join me in supporting a bold, community-led vision in Near North Minneapolis that prioritizes reparative justice and racial equity. Right now, Our Streets—a local transportation justice organization—is working with Near North residents to bring back 6th Avenue North and reclaim highway land that has polluted and divided the neighborhood for generations. Once called the “Beale Street of Minneapolis,” this area was a thriving Black and Jewish community until it was destroyed in 1939 to make way for Olson Highway—a highway to nowhere, built through the heart of a vibrant neighborhood. Olson Highway sees less traffic than narrower roadways like Broadway Avenue. Yet its intersections are the most dangerous in Minneapolis. That’s why Our Streets is leading the Bring Back 6th campaign: to remove the highway, restore the historic street grid, reconnect communities, and build a safer, more equitable future. And they have the unanimous support of the Minneapolis City Council. But here’s the challenge: The $1.6 million federal grant for this work is now at risk. Due to Trump’s executive orders, the agency overseeing their grant has been gutted. Now, more than $2 billion in Reconnecting Communities and Neighborhood Access and Equity grants are at risk, including two right here in Minnesota. This uncertainty puts serious financial strain on Our Streets and threatens delays — or even stoppage — to this historic work. These potential cuts, currently being debated in the federal budget reconciliation bill, would derail critical efforts across the country to repair past transportation harms and reconnect communities. This devastating decision cuts off future opportunities for communities like Near North to access federal support for reparative infrastructure projects, making this work more urgent than ever. Bringing back 6th could provide immense economic benefits, housing opportunities, reconnection and safer streets, alternative transportation options, and improved public health. After decades of disinvestment, Near North Minneapolis deserves a street that reflects the needs and dreams of its community—not outdated, dangerous infrastructure. Let’s Bring Back 6th. Let’s get this done. In Service, . You can also . Submit this form to Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **. Ilhan for Congress PO Box 33079 Washington D.C., 20033

How the Governor and President raised taxes on working people.

# [Food or Heat?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=170534237&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNTM0MjM3LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ3NDk1NzYsImV4cCI6MTc1NzM0MTU3NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.G_9K_-fbTadWTh1fCrzipZkTM1OdZ1RW2NHm_dO9CEs)
### How the Governor and President raised taxes on working people.
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Aug 9
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=170534237&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNTM0MjM3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NDc0OTU3NiwiZXhwIjoxNzU3MzQxNTc2LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.uuVDiI-fYpxkTtWrtK7aHHnJTFQ7iv9VwwlzdMfBtzU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

**Today, Marilyn shows how the Missouri Governor, the legislature, the President, and Congress have acted to transfer massive amounts of wealth from the working poor to the utility companies who funded their campaigns.**
* * *
During Lucas' US Senate run, many people asked me why I decided to join him on the campaign trail all over Missouri. After all, not every spouse is interested, or thrilled, about the constant travel, the late nights, and the sacrifices a family makes to cover every corner of the state and meet as many people as possible. On top of that, at the beginning I was pregnant with our little boy Harvey. Later, we had a newborn to care for, I was juggling my master's degree, and I still had a full-time job. On paper, it probably looked like the worst timing in the world. But my answer was always the same: once you've seen what I saw across this state, you can't unsee it, and when the people in power don't care about the struggle and suffering, it's the job of the rest of us to pick up the slack.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.CSTFY2k23g-NDF9NnBDUItxNWBs1K3xTd73kGOzJw-k?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=170534237)
On our travels I saw, up close, how Missouri leaders were letting their campaign funders strip our state and the people here, who I'd come to love, for parts. The people they were destroying, senior citizens, mothers, workers, young adults, children, frequently came to us with tears in their eyes about situations they couldn't control and couldn't overcome.
In our travels, I really fell in love with Missouri and Missourians. With the farmland and the people who work it. With the stories shared over kitchen tables and in folding chairs at town halls. I fell in love with the stories, the conversations, the connections we made across the state. The more I listened, the more I knew: I wasn't going to walk away from them. That just isn't in me.
I've mentioned in past posts that I grew up on a farm, and I'm not trying to repeat myself here, but I can't leave behind the fact that my heart sank each time we visited rural communities and felt how deep the worry and sadness ran. You could see the weight in people's faces, not just worry, but the kind of quiet desperation that comes from feeling you've been doing everything right, and still the deck is stacked against you.
And that sinking feeling is about to get much worse throughout our state because one of the things that people brought up the most, their inability to pay their utility bills, is about to get even more impossible thanks to the people in charge of our state and country.
In Missouri, our newly elected governor, Mike Kehoe, alongside his pals in the state legislature, pushed through **Senate Bill 4**, an [energy package that critics warn will raise costs for families while cushioning utility profits](https://substack.com/redirect/3f3b1bea-9244-42b7-a8a2-afd9321a7805?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Under SB 4, utilities can now pass the cost of building new power plants onto customers, even before those plants are up and running, a practice known as **Construction Work in Progress** , which voters banned back in 1976. Despite the risks, Governor Kehoe signed it on April 9, 2025, under the promise of "securing Missouri's energy future," even as consumer advocates warned it would drive household bills up by as much as **$1,115 a year**.
At the federal level, this past April, RFK Jr. [fired the entire Missouri staff](https://substack.com/redirect/da020720-b958-4f86-a24e-764b126cc52c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program, otherwise known as LIHEAP. A program that [helped about 216,000 Missourians pay their utility bills in 2023](https://substack.com/redirect/8b41cf5e-5431-478a-a1cc-8a1edad875e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and also provides funding to weatherize and make homes more energy efficient, saving us all. And yet the President's FY26 Budget for LIHEAP proposes [eliminating $4 billion in assistance](https://substack.com/redirect/b5e2618d-46e5-4ad9-a70f-a57b36e4973d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to about 6 million very low-income households that rely on LIHEAP to pay their home heating and cooling bills.
This hits Missouri hard, across just the eight counties [Central Missouri Community Action, a LIHEA service provider, serves, for example, nearly 14,000 individuals in nearly 5,000 households utilized LIHEAP funds to keep lights on and temperature controlled.](https://substack.com/redirect/50b5920e-fd04-431d-a99f-784133b4833f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) The majority of them are people with disabilities or elderly households. This comes at the worst possible time considering that one in six families are already behind on their energy bills. For them, it comes down to choosing between groceries, medicine, and the power bill.
Katrina Metzler, executive director of the National Energy & Utility Affordability Coalition, summed it up bluntly: "My fear is that quietly in their homes, grandmothers will die this summer"." As for winter, there is no indication of anyone new being hired to run the program or get utility funding to those who are in danger without it.
**But it doesn 't end there.**
Passed on July 4, The One Big Beautiful Bill Act makes sweeping changes to federal energy policy that benefit fossil fuel interests while reversing investments in energy efficiency and renewables.
[Here is what the bill means for energy efficiency and energy costs](https://substack.com/redirect/4433ce16-4daf-4293-aa34-8c72d39d81eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in a nutshell:
* The law is expected to reduce U.S. electricity generation capacity by 340 gigawatts by 2035, _making it harder_ to meet growing demand and weakening American manufacturing competitiveness.
* Wholesale **electricity prices are projected to increase 25 percent by 2030 and 74 percent by 2035.**
* Consumer electricity rates are projected to increase 9-18% by 2035, pushing average household energy bills up.
* Missouri households may face [annual costs of up to $640 by 2035 ](https://substack.com/redirect/3df981b6-33a2-460a-b88f-25eb2a8d6ad0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* National economic output is also expected to suffer. The law would _reduce_ U.S. gross domestic product by $980 billion cumulatively by 2035.
* Workers are projected to _lose_ 760,000 jobs by 2030, particularly in industries supported by clean energy manufacturing and construction.
* Immediate phasing‐out of clean energy tax credits for solar, wind, batteries, and efficiency programs, undermining new clean generation. This slows the addition of new power to the grid and [saddles customers with rising costs](https://substack.com/redirect/9ae64320-4652-46a3-809a-188dc02faa12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

The corporate gains and moving from renewable energy are costing us, nearly 60 utilities across the country have already raised or proposed rate hikes in 2025 alone, representing $38.3 billion in electricity customer costs and $3.5 billion in gas customer hikes, amounting to 56.7 million electric households and 26 million gas customers impacted. In other words, while claiming to cut taxes, the Missouri Governor and President Trump are actually massively increasing taxes for the middle class and poor through their utility bills, and transferring that money directly to fossil fuel and utility company shareholders.
It's an American tragedy, and we could have so much better.
One thing that never failed to excite the crowd was Lucas's _[The Marshall Plan for the Midwest](https://substack.com/redirect/9c6b6e4a-f4e7-424d-92a4-d6887adb4175?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. It was his way of thinking through how we could keep our region on the map by investing in the build-out and production of the next generation of energy right here in Missouri, to create good jobs, improve the efficiency of older buildings and homes, and save everyday people money. The ideas were practical, things that could help lower utility bills, modernize what we already have, and give families a little breathing room instead of watching their paychecks vanish into outdated systems.
The plan was based on a knowledge of Missouri and what it is really like here on the ground where older homes are harder to keep cool in the summer and warm in the winter, and there are more of them in historically red-lined neighborhoods.
The "[St. Louis Energy Burden Report](https://substack.com/redirect/6c676634-56d6-4ea7-83ee-af87e2c6383a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" dives into census tracts across the state, using real data from Missouri utility bills to understand how costs overlap with factors like race, income and even rates of childhood asthma.
The report found that residents with an average income of about $35,000 pay $2,408 a year for utilities, while residents with an average income of about $85,000 pay $2,082 a year, according to the report. Meaning [that low-income Missourians pay more utilities than wealthier counterparts](https://substack.com/redirect/58127478-1e92-4847-95f8-bfccdb65047c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which seems counterintuitive since higher income people usually have larger houses. But it happens because people who are lower-income live in older more broken-down housing, which is significantly less efficient. Since lower-income people are also more likely to be renters who pay their own utilities but don't own their homes, the decisions about energy efficiency or appliances in their homes aren't up to them, but to their landlords.
If you live anywhere in the Midwest, you've felt it already. Your energy bill is climbing fast. A big part of that is [that heat waves are becoming more dangerous](https://substack.com/redirect/d3e0c4a1-4155-4aaf-af7a-103f62b7af50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), especially here in Missouri, where summer [temperatures have been climbing steadily over the years](https://substack.com/redirect/59960bb0-97de-428b-aed2-c46be826c5b5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But another part of that is the reason that the tech bros have buddied up so much with President Trump and the Republican Party: their insatiable thirst for energy.
[U.S. electricity demand, after being flat for nearly two decades, is projected to grow](https://substack.com/redirect/68663221-8c54-4fb0-a97e-af3b0476a649?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at about 1.7% per year through 2026, with commercial and industrial demand rising faster. New data centers, especially those serving [AI and cloud infrastructure, now consume 4.4% of U.S. electricity](https://substack.com/redirect/77776fe7-1147-49e3-9ff0-590739b4bc96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and are expected to _triple_ by 2028, according to the US Department of Energy. And, of course, much of that cost is being picked up by the public, the working poor, subsidizing the Big Tech's power bill.
New power plants like the [natural gas plants](https://substack.com/redirect/606e30f0-0271-4871-a182-440af2e74203?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that some utilities are proposing to serve new data centers, are extremely expensive to build. And when build new plants, expand old ones, build transmission lines, or update grids for AI or data centers, the costs are largely passed on to everyday ratepayers.
For example, In Louisiana, customers of the state's major public utility could be responsible for [$5 billion](https://substack.com/redirect/59ab7a52-0719-4512-a8bb-ab4112238ecf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in costs associated with new natural gas plants and transmission lines needed to serve a Meta data center.
And as we all know, the current administration is doubling down on the outdated energy sources, [relying on oil and gas to power AI as the future of fossil fuels](https://substack.com/redirect/9f35ef1c-9a58-4a2f-bda3-c0985fa32f0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Putting it together, older, inefficient homes coupled with more extreme weather leave residents exposed to higher bills. Rolling back energy-efficient upgrades and solar incentives removes the tools many families use to reduce costs. Utility monopolies ensure rate increases are passed directly to consumers without relief. And for regions like Missouri, the combined effect may mean extra hundreds of dollars per household per year by 2030-2035, while local economies suffer job losses.
Midwestern families are being squeezed at the meter while big energy flows upward.
And our job has to be to connect the dots for people who don't understand how or why they have to choose between groceries and heat or cooling.
-Marilyn
