| | | Dear Friend, Your participation in Hillsdale College's **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** is requested.

Your response will only take a few minutes, but the subject is crucial to the future of liberty and the separation of powers in America.

The American Founders thought that the proper role of the Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution.

But progressives see the Court differently. They want activist judges to legislate from the bench.
## Do you agree that the role of the Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution as it is written and NOT to make laws or advance a particular policy agenda?
[YES](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/GyyrhdfdaJbcTAOwv2_MVS3VPzujShDygfFDu3zxSRUXpc2oVCTkyogwhTTRUvlZal1-bAmSe21IBRq3YxICjggtWZclS2i-cDaoqdARlNROi3RDMbrOhwrGIZcEMEaVqLahqKpjRSCauC8ubiB6klSGmlHcPrLJd1qlhLRqNq0ZXhDQJweprS5JtSoBh9uqBn-GW-8vs96AJHq5TACAcPwJA1VlGJ1TqIp0iQmUsQV0YQC7GXBTP5Egis5Fa2vrAsSyY_NgtDcBDqCaZK1MGOu59laFq0D_XvuToZfu6jPCL2amaBl1KKoNZXvQakpm12nTOmtUNGQOrsiL1r208VZqZYfHGpTPqCUHqVEbaxyL5dVl8jbchSWQd3m7Z8_ZjHqBGsz3mwqQR1xXk0RVTk5gdvnd0VOmm_8haJVpQY7Snm1mCU4GZ2-nlh-6Nu9wct3oQxlhTaIHHL65tKTRG3luqX8obtWvgBnLTEshUZOxrrKdSiuIKBAnzMCy3KeG5hmjIvHztlm348mxVm5ixG5EWjju9yQdzAInqjteGvJ6Za54QZUA1DVuFi6koAwHy8-yPJY-wT1VXu970a_skwUQgNXJZ3fY9ibhIPWtAhr8vzVYmLTnSU7PrxGHla4H0FmbhQP6KNdXk4bybRhNMJ208RPbtOdArkXyu7LXkymkqUkCuD-bQxLI2coUKXAaxMKU80P5RIwMdgA--AvsS1rBNyHr1c1VvA/agvwkTkaSR2Rx4ELGVDeQkU2ghi6m-YJ/11) [NO](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/rpFOg4hCotNtm4ju13DkuefI9HkG9QG_4jLwj-r8XHo8MidKYsAEB2GBnIc-vlNYHBkwSjU0I1MceeHG8qZJCnn5IE-hgMgkDuXmsPE-mmyZZ3BNOr68IDsL-X1NZNVQ7uWDDJFGosGPb6MTrXcGyKRz0koZEY4qUirukFSa8oD-LTMttCylt7g0Z_GpC4bmM3_sUraANfvy6YCjQEuQKuPZeqkAiuXlbE3R1-u2xxEzgy1azPl7MGv8RoclOU5xGrVorBWYRaNUlIXxJ43c4cD4VcFucRfmvRwQnVoieV8F87TnaruUvtkQjr7s2iK3u-URWcZT8All404jNBv4R12T9cL94g028FTz3_9l1OxJvkfLBaL69tqe-U9m-d0ld0oqC6Rpug8lwZ8nmdhjecaTrXyyfL4EcSeBfyei-z4mmEptzI2xtN5aG1gUcvLsh1uNpbqUntGnKINf9_48L8GGGsOdQlY29wKIZ-TYTWPVuee6uSf04vgwuTqi8d7oVds1KmMZ0ur-rZb2Z7iLruBwGnLg8zqEs3-8Z6Q8asJPKG78q7OEwYroKrfb9L8vczZ-wl4d2Ks8zW9x2-PYXFxHpuDlA2M1Y9ATaVPV_3akPH3AQfDvVqxjovI580JQdV7DB_20--I73f-yVc6ESMS-dK4np_SKsrJAsJIfHpwElvTLJcEIShz3lv-v-hwiwaB_bsiTwL7EuzkOr0sbUmicv6ibm_30jA/VEyhqgaAqZEhXBa7h4kyWVlpCANiJaYK/11) Even worse, progressives dismayed by recent Supreme Court decisions want to "pack" the bench with additional justices to advance a preferred policy agenda.

As Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) put it in a _Boston Globe_ op-ed: "To restore balance and integrity to a broken institution, Congress must expand the Supreme Court by four or more seats."

That is why I'm reaching out to you today...

_What is your opinion about the Supreme Court 's role in our constitutional republic? _

Please share your thoughts with me using the College's **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** right away.

Your response will help Hillsdale form effective strategies to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty--including the proper role of an independent judiciary that applies the Constitution instead of legislating from the bench.

You can easily complete your **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** using this secure link: **[https://info.hillsdale.edu/supreme-court-survey](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/KkfLCFh2adoPMHdbsP7zCtS_vi56vP9yx3S84IHVvwsIk0EdJrvOPb92Tg5FRCVS2F3kPE9Ao-k4Cjn5IuwwMCIocN9Sn8IFUamwsnEPHQASLE1msssZCtaKhQTDMcGDEQoP769ax33mNjt77Xrcvk-dp1GcjmoVzOVHEn-dtUM3FDckVyvJhducxufEtN4FxNDI9cI6KcT-mKVBRMzItL6ganrdIHoWihHgSW3xoZObely3XjibxPhb1Um7iU2cLi9rViY-P7bGtsMbudX2_zs1l7ZateTfRDqTK_z7XBtBrneXcFhLRZTduOsVj936_syOQinQXJnQe52ujIMyqdxV-56hr8YAogVSlnqL-0eILSaBXvZ_ODBJgiuFZydwUgBzufoo2U0HNIPQdOH0NeWeBF-uLFebi8Wm9dC6J4hh-GseXXcACidbrd9PevQbWgC-TAO0wTImkyXWT-HrjJWpvKbzHuLZBJWlf6Mt4JdFlGdTgpAyvDa2QfGWDNmrzUVeHZtQEsyLXUsjCgu1UUWwyE0JqmM7wR2hYBgjsFvcUpS9BV1RFb4W5kkdf45T7_uwHY4iTmI5zfctsBkji98qXvDZjQuiAOJWvGW3bOf0iIzHCjMH9R_yfu1ge7zE1hlAD3qy4s7Jjx0aQLytTicYl4fOQzZrvmuA_p7DGMCYLC0_trVyAN6qW3hvAfSyI9zS7Dr-Jzl6c9Szg8Vnx7Y4hjdXGaDTqw/Rku5rbKzS2JanGmDI_9FK3zA73Lcsq7g/11)** Thank you for your time and attention--and for your support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844

name: Christina Gagnier
subject: I will burn tires and tennis shoes to get this done.
body:
|
#
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113039622/562442114/1631137775?refcode=em-8.9.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODczMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWVlMDkxMi01Mzc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QO8snhM9REPS3OyxzAwM8nFqBOEVwbdhKRN0kwcPU0s=)

Here is what we know, Peter:

Donald Trump is a threat to our democracy and our communities.
My opponent Young Kim is a rubber stamp for Trump and his policies.

**In a race this competitive and consequential for CA-40 and the House majority, we need more than that – we need a vision for how we move forward for working families.**

I am determined to flip this seat and provide that vision for Orange County and the Inland Empire. And in this race, I believe I am the only candidate who has what it takes to get it done.

As a lawyer and as a former Chino Valley school board member, I have stood up to far-right extremism and bullies. For my actions, I have been threatened with a gun, stalked, and had my home broken into to intimidate me. But I did not back down. Because I know what it takes to get things done and solve problems – and that is what I will do in Congress.

But first, we need to fire Young Kim. She and the national GOP are in the business of taking opportunity away from folks – from health care to food assistance and beyond.

I believe that we should be providing opportunities for working families, not take them away. These ideas, big and small, WILL make an impact. I will criss-cross the entire district to spread my vision – I will burn tires, and I will burn tennis shoes from the miles we travel to get it done.

**[If you believe in my vision of opportunity and ideas big and small, please chip in $3 now. Any amount will move us closer to flipping this seat and help me reach voters with how I will work for them.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113039624/562442119/1631137775?refcode=em-8.9.25-all-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODczMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWVlMDkxMi01Mzc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QO8snhM9REPS3OyxzAwM8nFqBOEVwbdhKRN0kwcPU0s=)**

clean_body: | = Here is what we know, Peter: Donald Trump is a threat to our democracy and our communities. My opponent Young Kim is a rubber stamp for Trump and his policies. In a race this competitive and consequential for CA-40 and the House majority, we need more than that – we need a vision for how we move forward for working families. I am determined to flip this seat and provide that vision for Orange County and the Inland Empire. And in this race, I believe I am the only candidate who has what it takes to get it done. As a lawyer and as a former Chino Valley school board member, I have stood up to far-right extremism and bullies. For my actions, I have been threatened with a gun, stalked, and had my home broken into to intimidate me. But I did not back down. Because I know what it takes to get things done and solve problems – and that is what I will do in Congress. But first, we need to fire Young Kim. She and the national GOP are in the business of taking opportunity away from folks – from health care to food assistance and beyond. I believe that we should be providing opportunities for working families, not take them away. These ideas, big and small, WILL make an impact. I will criss-cross the entire district to spread my vision – I will burn tires, and I will burn tennis shoes from the miles we travel to get it done. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you, Christina | Christina Gagnier is a community leader, small business owner, former school board member, and privacy advocate defending Orange County and Inland Empire families. To support Christina's campaign = To volunteer on Christina's campaign, To receive fewer emails from Christina Gagnier's campaign, sign up for our option here. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Christina Gagnier for Congress. name: Cancer Fight
subject: They’re throwing away hope for millions
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ISAIAH MARTIN FOR CONGRESS
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/109386/images/IRM_wrapper-email.jpg]
friend, hey — it’s Isaiah Martin.
If you missed my recent video (or if you’ve managed to stay blissfully offline), here’s what I said:
Trump and RFK Jr . are cutting funding for mRNA research. That’s the technology scientists were using to develop a cancer preventer .
This one is personal for me. I’ve lost multiple aunts to cancer. And I know you or someone you love has likely felt that pain too. We were so close to a breakthrough. So close to something that could have given you 10 more years with your parents, more memories with your spouse, or maybe even the chance to live cancer-free.
Now? Years of progress are gone because Republican senators were too weak to stand up to Trump, RFK Jr., and the anti-science fringe they cater to.
When I get to Congress, I’ll lead the fight to bring back this funding. I can’t undo the time we’ve lost, but I will not let their cruelty and cowardice cost us more.
Help Power the Fight Every dollar keeps organizers on the doors, lawyers in the room, and this campaign moving. If you’re with us, chip in now.
Donate → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/klemail_imartin] Secure ActBlue • Your support makes a real impact

For every family who’s been touched by cancer, we can’t give up.
In the fight,
Isaiah Martin
Texas’s 18th Congressional District

---

You may recall that on the campaign trail we raised the issue of I-95 and Traffic on the busiest corridor of the 4th Congressional District.
The article was titled - **[Jim Himes Loves Honey - But Connecticut Contractors Need the Money -](https://substack.com/redirect/d780b4cd-400d-4072-85e3-cead8f64f473?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[Voters Raise Concern about use of out-of-state contractors from New York](https://substack.com/redirect/d780b4cd-400d-4072-85e3-cead8f64f473?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Pledge your support](https://substack..KQoXEyaKPsBaN-ivHbS6zGhXQqkTELbkR8QCM4IYqKI?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=170370146)
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1454853&post_id=170370146&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMzcwMTQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ3NjY2NjIsImV4cCI6MTc1NzM1ODY2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE0NTQ4NTMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aybGVB6BtWvG_LcKLJDPWqt2AZV-NgktO4xy04eiswk)
The issue was that all of these contracts for the highways (funded with State and Federal Dollars) were going to YONKERS Contracting Company -
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/48e68f3f-b6e9-4705-b722-320cf5aa3ea9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Photo- Tyler Fedor [Source](https://substack.com/redirect/ba2329b0-4de5-4823-8b88-5cdd4a7cff25?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Conveniently on the groundbreaking project, CT Republican elected officials were left out of this ceremony, including State Senator Ryan Fazio - who has been a champion to fight against highway dollar waste on CT Roads and the exorbitant costs per mile for Connecticut highway repair which rank as some of the highest in the nation. _**Senator Fazio has just filed for an exploratory committee for his hat in the ring to run for Governor of this Constitution State Next Year. His Gubernatorial Exploratory Page is[Here](https://substack.com/redirect/a3f8cd0b-6788-47d4-89f1-a0033daeb342?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
As for this construction conundrum, we were so disgusted that we sent an inquiry that was never responded to. That inquiry is reproduced at the end of this. It addressed logical issues, including the benefits to CT from Income Tax revenue and impact to local businesses along with the greenhouse gas from transporting equipment from New York to CT etc...
**Connecticut Pays and Yonkers Benefits - AGAIN**
Well Yonkers is back at it on the Merritt (in addition to the sign changes by a Farmington Connecticut Based Firm - Ducci Electrical Contracting). For More on that click [Here ](https://substack.com/redirect/97bf111f-2b8d-41df-a848-0b4cd2851bcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)\-
As a State run by one-party and supported by the unions that get paid to volunteer (not picket) on state elections, should they be more concerned about jobs awarded to out-of-state contractors. _**Why should they fight for higher wages and benefits when funds leave the state to our neighbor New York? Almost every large project that we have recently looked at (especially those with a federal component) are won by out-of-state contractors.**_
If you are stuck during this resurfacing project through December - Blame Yonkers. But at least they claim these roads will last longer and driver safety will improve as:
"The roadway will have an overlay that will extend the lifespan of the wearing surface. Specific curves along the mainline roadway will have a High Friction Surface Treatment to help reduce accidents in those areas." [See CT DOT Press Release](https://substack.com/redirect/c5d1fe6a-8736-4291-a10d-707360519bdd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**What is the Union Stance of Connecticut Projects?**
While Connecticut does not have "union-only" mandates for all highway projects, **Project Labor Agreements (PLAs)** are increasingly being utilized on certain state and federally funded highway construction projects.
However, Under the Biden Administration - PLAs are required on direct federal construction projects of $35 million or more, with limited exceptions with [Executive Order 14063](https://substack.com/redirect/e4fd965b-9f57-4419-ad87-8dafdc18d590?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that was signed on February 4, 2022.
PLAs increase the cost of construction significantly
In 2020, the Beacon Hill Institute issued a study "The Effect of Project Labor Agreements on Public School Construction in Connecticut." The study concluded that Connecticut schools built under these PLAs cost "19.8% more than schools that were bid and constructed through fair and open competition, free from PLA requirements."
According to the Connecticut Chapter of the Associated Builders and Contractors, approximately 85 percent of the Connecticut construction industry choose to not join a union and these PLAs disqualify Nutmegger's that are qualified to work on private sector projects are disqualified to work on projects that are paid for by their own tax dollars. [See CT ABC Site](https://substack.com/redirect/c172f351-45a9-476f-9ea2-c4bcc425570c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
If PLAs are not working to serve Connecticut taxpayer and keep Nutmeggers employed, then the PLAs, if not eliminated, should at least be adjusted so Connecticut Contractors can be eligible for projects here in Connecticut.
_**Governor Lamont is utilizing a union-only Project Labor Agreement on the Gold Star Bridge (Connecticut 's Largest Bridge) - This is a $158 million facelift over the next 6 years. [Source](https://substack.com/redirect/a125409b-d4c0-457e-8b62-6f5f8d98212a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_
Time to stop watching CT tax dollars flow to New York or Mass, RI, PA etc.
* For those in the middle of the state - The Connecticut Department of Transportation (CTDOT) bridge rehabilitation on I-91 southbound in Hartford. (project 0063-0726) to replace the bridge deck, joints, membrane and overlay, parapets and roadway lighting and make roadway improvements just south of the I-84 / I-91 interchange was awarded to J.F. White Contracting, Inc. at a cost of $70,544,422.00 - **This Contractor is Massachusetts based**

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/25f986c7-9882-477b-adf5-e284d7120bb4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source J.F. White Contracting Website](https://substack.com/redirect/ca6d40d4-586c-4cc6-aeba-93032d1f8a46?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**Federal Challenges to Federal Union Mandate**
On the Federal Level - PLAs are under scrutiny as Judge Ryan T. Holte of the U.S. Court of Federal Claims [ruled](https://substack.com/redirect/16d42b3f-e37b-4371-9eed-129e7a6520ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on Jan. 19, 2025, in favor of federal contractors who challenged requirements for collective bargaining agreement with labor organizations regarding employment for all large-scale construction projects.[1](https://substack.com/redirect/0e97f126-649e-43fa-87ad-196ad92a9c43?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**Moreover, President Trump** is revoking many of the Biden-Era Executive Orders and we will see if Trump revokes EO 14063, Use of Project Labor Agreements for Federal Construction Projects.
**WHERE DO WE GO FROM HERE CONNECTICUT?**
**It is time to Eliminate the PLAs or change them drastically. The Connecticut Chapter of the Associated Builders and Contractors** is a trade association dedicated to serving construction professionals throughout the Connecticut area. They proclaim that they are "the recognized advocate and defender of merit shop contractors and we address the unique challenges of our members and the industry."
**PLAs Eliminate 80 Percent of the CT Construction Workforce?**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d7727c57-c95b-466c-b70b-cbaba882fb6d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Source - ABC CT](https://substack.com/redirect/c172f351-45a9-476f-9ea2-c4bcc425570c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The PLAs are to blame and massive reform, especially as federal PLAs are being challenged, is necessary here in CT and wherever they are used.
_**If You are in Construction and Know anyone in Construction - Share this Article and Share to every taxpayer of Connecticut - Time to Stand Up and keep CT tax dollars in CT.**_
**ENOUGH IS ENOUGH**
* * *
This is the Inquiry Sent:
Dear Editors and Investigative Journalists of Connecticut's Major News & Media Outlets and Other Independent News Channel Outlets:
The Doctor Michael Goldstein for Congress Campaign for the 4th Congressional District has received numerous inquiries and concerns about Yonkers Contracting as the Primary Contractor for most CT DOT projects throughout Fairfield County and other parts of the state.
We see these trucks everywhere every night along the campaign trail when the roads are being worked on for improvements.
The concerns they raise are seeing New York License plates and why these are not Connecticut trucks.

Have you looked into:
1. How are these contracts awarded?
2. If an out-of-state contractor is hired, will it use Connecticut Union Construction workers and other ancillary Connecticut residents to participate in the construction or are all these workers New York as well?
3. If an out-of-state contractor is hired, will it use local suppliers of various materials or are all these suppliers New York as well?
4. Whether all contracts are awarded to the lowest bidder?
5. Are local contractors considered if their bid is slightly higher?
6. How is a contractor rewarded or penalized by its previous project for change order usage as the winners always seem to be the same?
7. The percentage of payrolls to Connecticut residents (if any)?
8. The benefit to local communities and economies in Connecticut if a local contractor is awarded the contract?
9. The benefits of contractor profits being re-invested into the local economy rather than exporting profits outside of CT for the out-of-state contractor and its out-of-state suppliers?
10. Additional Fees such as fuel surcharges and other distance based issues on logistics?

As Climate Change and Health Hazards associated with slow-moving traffic are also major concerns, are the following also included in the calculation:
1. The environmental impact of greenhouse gas based on travel distance of workers and equipment out of state to the project site?
2. The additional congestion of the workforce commuting to the construction site and how it will disrupt traffic for commuters?

We have been concerned about the I-95 traffic and the challenges to the Fairfield County corridor and now a project is starting. We saw the Yonkers Contracting banner and also wondered why there are no Connecticut Companies that win these bids.
While most of the highway funds are from the Federal Government, it is still Connecticut taxpayer funds as CT has traditionally received only 70 cents back on each federal dollar.
As a candidate for Congress in Connecticut and campaigning on improving the Fairfield County corridor, it is not just about improving the roads, but having a lasting impact on the local economies of Fairfield County and surrounding counties that keep federally spent dollars in Connecticut.
Some Links we have briefly assembled as this is really investigative reporting:
[https://www.thehour.com/news/article/i-95-delays-norwalk-westport-ctdot-bridge-replace-18404086.php](https://substack.com/redirect/a870c502-642a-4930-b69a-f248cfe9a610?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[https://www.thehour.com/news/article/norwalk-i-95-fairfield-avenue-bridge-fire-crash-19525562.php](https://substack.com/redirect/ee0aff37-657c-4497-a249-89ed7de78e44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[https://www.yonkerscontractingco.com/stamford-parking](https://substack.com/redirect/2eed8141-5484-463e-b973-2efaeb228200?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[https://newyork.news12.com/sick-of-backups-on-i-95-new-project-promises-relief](https://substack.com/redirect/c79ad8dc-996b-4f33-bd0d-30e387f0b137?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[https://www.ctinsider.com/projects/2024/news/ct-norwalk-bridge-i95-demolition/](https://substack.com/redirect/bfd3b955-a302-4fbd-a34e-8bcc9181f025?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The voters want to know why tax dollars leave for out-of-state contractors when they know there are contractors in the State (not necessarily in the Congressional District) to do the work.
We would appreciate your prompt attention to this matter as this is a major concern for our Voters.
[](https://substack.com/profile/45119203-jonathan-goldstein)| A guest post by| [Jonathan Goldstein](https://substack.com/@jonathangoldstein81?utm_campaign=guest_post_bio&utm_medium=email)Business Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 [Subscribe to Jonathan](https://substack.com/redirect/1a1f4a44-9f7e-4fe4-845c-89374123df45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

clean_body: How CT Highway Dollars are Going to Out-of-State Firms - is the PLA to Blame? | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Goldstein For Congress - Please Subscribe and also visit us at www.goldsteinforcongress.com How CT Highway Dollars are Going to Out-of-State Firms - is the PLA to Blame? | | and | Aug 9 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | You may recall that on the campaign trail we raised the issue of I-95 and Traffic on the busiest corridor of the 4th Congressional District. The article was titled - The issue was that all of these contracts for the highways (funded with State and Federal Dollars) were going to YONKERS Contracting Company - | | ---|---|--- Photo- Tyler Fedor Conveniently on the groundbreaking project, CT Republican elected officials were left out of this ceremony, including State Senator Ryan Fazio - who has been a champion to fight against highway dollar waste on CT Roads and the exorbitant costs per mile for Connecticut highway repair which rank as some of the highest in the nation. Senator Fazio has just filed for an exploratory committee for his hat in the ring to run for Governor of this Constitution State Next Year. His Gubernatorial Exploratory Page is As for this construction conundrum, we were so disgusted that we sent an inquiry that was never responded to. That inquiry is reproduced at the end of this. It addressed logical issues, including the benefits to CT from Income Tax revenue and impact to local businesses along with the greenhouse gas from transporting equipment from New York to CT etc... Connecticut Pays and Yonkers Benefits - AGAIN Well Yonkers is back at it on the Merritt (in addition to the sign changes by a Farmington Connecticut Based Firm - Ducci Electrical Contracting). For More on that click \- As a State run by one-party and supported by the unions that get paid to volunteer (not picket) on state elections, should they be more concerned about jobs awarded to out-of-state contractors. Why should they fight for higher wages and benefits when funds leave the state to our neighbor New York? Almost every large project that we have recently looked at (especially those with a federal component) are won by out-of-state contractors. If you are stuck during this resurfacing project through December - Blame Yonkers. But at least they claim these roads will last longer and driver safety will improve as: "The roadway will have an overlay that will extend the lifespan of the wearing surface. Specific curves along the mainline roadway will have a High Friction Surface Treatment to help reduce accidents in those areas." What is the Union Stance of Connecticut Projects? While Connecticut does not have "union-only" mandates for all highway projects, Project Labor Agreements (PLAs) are increasingly being utilized on certain state and federally funded highway construction projects. However, Under the Biden Administration - PLAs are required on direct federal construction projects of $35 million or more, with limited exceptions with that was signed on February 4, 2022. PLAs increase the cost of construction significantly In 2020, the Beacon Hill Institute issued a study "The Effect of Project Labor Agreements on Public School Construction in Connecticut." The study concluded that Connecticut schools built under these PLAs cost "19.8% more than schools that were bid and constructed through fair and open competition, free from PLA requirements." According to the Connecticut Chapter of the Associated Builders and Contractors, approximately 85 percent of the Connecticut construction industry choose to not join a union and these PLAs disqualify Nutmegger's that are qualified to work on private sector projects are disqualified to work on projects that are paid for by their own tax dollars. If PLAs are not working to serve Connecticut taxpayer and keep Nutmeggers employed, then the PLAs, if not eliminated, should at least be adjusted so Connecticut Contractors can be eligible for projects here in Connecticut. Governor Lamont is utilizing a union-only Project Labor Agreement on the Gold Star Bridge (Connecticut 's Largest Bridge) - This is a $158 million facelift over the next 6 years. Time to stop watching CT tax dollars flow to New York or Mass, RI, PA etc. For those in the middle of the state - The Connecticut Department of Transportation (CTDOT) bridge rehabilitation on I-91 southbound in Hartford. (project 0063-0726) to replace the bridge deck, joints, membrane and overlay, parapets and roadway lighting and make roadway improvements just south of the I-84 / I-91 interchange was awarded to J.F. White Contracting, Inc. at a cost of $70,544,422.00 - This Contractor is Massachusetts based | | ---|---|--- Federal Challenges to Federal Union Mandate On the Federal Level - PLAs are under scrutiny as Judge Ryan T. Holte of the U.S. Court of Federal Claims on Jan. 19, 2025, in favor of federal contractors who challenged requirements for collective bargaining agreement with labor organizations regarding employment for all large-scale construction projects. Moreover, President Trump is revoking many of the Biden-Era Executive Orders and we will see if Trump revokes EO 14063, Use of Project Labor Agreements for Federal Construction Projects. WHERE DO WE GO FROM HERE CONNECTICUT? It is time to Eliminate the PLAs or change them drastically. The Connecticut Chapter of the Associated Builders and Contractors is a trade association dedicated to serving construction professionals throughout the Connecticut area. They proclaim that they are "the recognized advocate and defender of merit shop contractors and we address the unique challenges of our members and the industry." PLAs Eliminate 80 Percent of the CT Construction Workforce? | | ---|---|--- The PLAs are to blame and massive reform, especially as federal PLAs are being challenged, is necessary here in CT and wherever they are used. If You are in Construction and Know anyone in Construction - Share this Article and Share to every taxpayer of Connecticut - Time to Stand Up and keep CT tax dollars in CT. ENOUGH IS ENOUGH * This is the Inquiry Sent: Dear Editors and Investigative Journalists of Connecticut's Major News & Media Outlets and Other Independent News Channel Outlets: The Doctor Michael Goldstein for Congress Campaign for the 4th Congressional District has received numerous inquiries and concerns about Yonkers Contracting as the Primary Contractor for most CT DOT projects throughout Fairfield County and other parts of the state. We see these trucks everywhere every night along the campaign trail when the roads are being worked on for improvements. The concerns they raise are seeing New York License plates and why these are not Connecticut trucks. Have you looked into: 1. How are these contracts awarded? 2. If an out-of-state contractor is hired, will it use Connecticut Union Construction workers and other ancillary Connecticut residents to participate in the construction or are all these workers New York as well? 3. If an out-of-state contractor is hired, will it use local suppliers of various materials or are all these suppliers New York as well? 4. Whether all contracts are awarded to the lowest bidder? 5. Are local contractors considered if their bid is slightly higher? 6. How is a contractor rewarded or penalized by its previous project for change order usage as the winners always seem to be the same? 7. The percentage of payrolls to Connecticut residents (if any)? 8. The benefit to local communities and economies in Connecticut if a local contractor is awarded the contract? 9. The benefits of contractor profits being re-invested into the local economy rather than exporting profits outside of CT for the out-of-state contractor and its out-of-state suppliers? 10. Additional Fees such as fuel surcharges and other distance based issues on logistics? As Climate Change and Health Hazards associated with slow-moving traffic are also major concerns, are the following also included in the calculation: 1. The environmental impact of greenhouse gas based on travel distance of workers and equipment out of state to the project site? 2. The additional congestion of the workforce commuting to the construction site and how it will disrupt traffic for commuters? We have been concerned about the I-95 traffic and the challenges to the Fairfield County corridor and now a project is starting. We saw the Yonkers Contracting banner and also wondered why there are no Connecticut Companies that win these bids. While most of the highway funds are from the Federal Government, it is still Connecticut taxpayer funds as CT has traditionally received only 70 cents back on each federal dollar. As a candidate for Congress in Connecticut and campaigning on improving the Fairfield County corridor, it is not just about improving the roads, but having a lasting impact on the local economies of Fairfield County and surrounding counties that keep federally spent dollars in Connecticut. Some Links we have briefly assembled as this is really investigative reporting: The voters want to know why tax dollars leave for out-of-state contractors when they know there are contractors in the State (not necessarily in the Congressional District) to do the work. We would appreciate your prompt attention to this matter as this is a major concern for our Voters. | A guest post by| Consultant, Political Consultant, Campaign Manager, Construction Consultant and Corporate Attorney. Jonathan served as the Campaign Manager for Dr. Michael Goldstein - CT-04 The Goldstein Substack is free today. But if you enjoyed this post, you can tell The Goldstein Substack that their writing is valuable by pledging a future subscription. You won't be charged unless they enable payments. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Michael Goldstein ? ?

name: Seth Magaziner
subject: I want to hear your good ideas.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress
body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=D37LGcr9usyzzp9y3V8BWxVyTi8FJP1o8dy5XJKyCnkLesLwe1M83FYqjV8UB3Ia14n0Wb2C%2F8sXzNAlSgr1MJ%2Fe%2Fk8agx%2FYsz%2B5P5FFC6XdUPnpu6KOyfKtJM4hvbzf1Z%2FPlIC%2FRZykPBbNeCRNof8laXoW5A5cxr4dAQqpwyASlMY3FZhq2kOkzRyYE30SrovVxg6nQgu4PbfZl8LueHWpz3R%2Fjk30qgObYsrdKqSoGNNAOj%2B5LJW6SrhOVWc5t9G0UOFf3414TMk2pftTM4MJjc8LpYPdqOx6s%2B5SvzK6s2OE%2BhpbNg9CK64WJs9Trn1wUT5OYcIOtX%2Bl1CkgDtBVTBQZ%2FBTrGzFLMZItFpbikUiXgvkbkAz7DPf3wBVPc9vFsd9k9Bv3LqDSQMeXRkUUpesINurioyx%2F9c1YqgP%2B2L73F3gtK8TIT53yIUM1)
---
| |
---
Hi Peter,

If there’s one thing I believe about public service, it’s this: the best leaders are the best listeners.

**That’s why today, I’m asking for your voice.**

I'm launching a new initiative to spotlight the issues and ideas that matter most to Rhode Islanders — **and I want your input._[If there’s a challenge your family is facing, or a policy you think could make life better for working people, I want to hear it.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113040673/562445455/1447405018?source=EM_NBI_SM_20250809_SV_GoodIdeas_0_6MO_SethMagaziner_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWI3ZWJhZi01NTc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fvouSeFpkXs2qQvNmyfAYT26SnETSLu7n-znS3G8ojs=&emci=25db98a9-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=beb7ebaf-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15384964)_** | | [TELL ME WHAT YOU THINK](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113040674/562445456/1447405018?source=EM_NBI_SM_20250809_SV_GoodIdeas_0_6MO_SethMagaziner_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWI3ZWJhZi01NTc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fvouSeFpkXs2qQvNmyfAYT26SnETSLu7n-znS3G8ojs=&emci=25db98a9-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=beb7ebaf-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15384964)
---
**My focus isn’t on lobbyists or billionaires, it’s about you.** It's about families working multiple jobs to stay afloat, seniors who need stronger Social Security benefits, and young people who deserve a future without crushing debt.

But to keep pushing forward, I need to know what matters most to you. **_[Will you take two minutes to fill out our survey and help shape the path ahead?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113040675/562445457/1447405018?source=EM_NBI_SM_20250809_SV_GoodIdeas_0_6MO_SethMagaziner_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWI3ZWJhZi01NTc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fvouSeFpkXs2qQvNmyfAYT26SnETSLu7n-znS3G8ojs=&emci=25db98a9-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=beb7ebaf-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15384964)_**
| [TAKE THE OFFICIAL SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113040676/562445458/1447405018?source=EM_NBI_SM_20250809_SV_GoodIdeas_0_6MO_SethMagaziner_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZWI3ZWJhZi01NTc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fvouSeFpkXs2qQvNmyfAYT26SnETSLu7n-znS3G8ojs=&emci=25db98a9-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=beb7ebaf-5575-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15384964)

Let’s build something better.

subject: Chuck Schumer is SOUNDING THE ALARM (!!!)
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU) **\- BREAKING FROM _ABC_ -
[SENATE DEMOCRATS TRY TO FORCE DOJ TO RELEASE EPSTEIN FILES USING LITTLE-KNOWN LAW](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=3&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)** [**We need to prove that Americans across the country want to see these files released. So please, friend, sign on now to stand with Chuck Schumer and Senate Democrats:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=4&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=5&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
| [TELL THE DOJ: RELEASE THE FILES! >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=6&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
---
**50,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED BEFORE 9:00AM EST ON 08/10/2025**
Senator Chuck Schumer just made a historic move to FORCE the Department of Justice to release every single Epstein file.

[He used a rare emergency power — the Rule of Five — to compel the DOJ to hand over decades of hidden evidence, names, communications, and deals.](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=7&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
[](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=8&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
For decades, the wealthy and well-connected have buried the truth. They’ve used their power and privilege to protect predators and silence victims.

Now, they’re running scared — **because if Sen. Schumer succeeds, their secrets will finally be dragged into the light.**

But Sen. Schumer won’t have an easy fight. He’ll face backlash from MAGA Republicans, far-right operatives, and billionaires (like Donald Trump) desperate to keep their names out of the headlines.

That’s where we come in.
[**We’re launching an emergency petition to show that the American people are with Sen. Schumer, demanding transparency and justice. So please, sign on now to stand with him:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=9&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
[TELL THE DOJ: RELEASE THE FILES! >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65760?t=10&akid=23276%2E4449941%2E4e16OU)
---
The stakes have never been higher, friend...

Fight for Reform

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: WATCH: What my residents think about the Big Budget Betrayal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
This bill hands a $90,000 annual tax cut to households making over $1 million a year while working people get roughly $40. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWGtB3sPBt03qJHb_ayRVMGvVB-zhdMPdyJDL3ajhzxFsstch_VW2z0FU8XK5cQrKtLKh640PWdxkCk5ykVj0Zl5R5nCdbAZzwb3-OD1_g5ATYzdTJ57rpWpI2nhKmsJg5eG-2w0tlu1zEB1x1iXX_gmAyKry5oR5apkICgfBOiNRHm74I97aWmXCvoUAoiGc09bqH_o3cJboj3dAyvUBW2Zne9KiGHic32ZqxMB3Z7Ac/4iw/GvkD_tYDTia9pk7Zb4zhuw/h0/h001.cGIXvEHkEnyIETkxWHM3M8MADaYCRgSS2mQ1GHg_hQA)
---
Friend, Republicans are hoping that everyday people don’t know what’s in the Big Budget Betrayal bill. They better be ready for a rude awakening. I recently spoke with residents in my district to hear what they had to say about this disastrous new law. They called it _"reverse Robin Hood."_ They said, _"the billionaires need to share the wealth and stop being so greedy."_ And, when talking about how SNAP cuts are taking food from our kids, they said, _"Why wouldn't you want our kids to be the biggest and the best?"_ The cuts Republicans made are so deep that 15 million people are expected to lose their health insurance and 51,000 people will die preventable deaths each year. And why? To fund trillions of dollars in tax cuts for the rich and corporations, and power Trump’s illegal, inhumane mass deportation scheme. Together, we’re saying hell no! [**Click here to watch this one-minute video to hear what my residents are saying about the Big Budget Betrayal, then donate today to power our fight to undo the largest cuts to healthcare in U.S. history and undo the tax cuts that enrich billionaires at our expense.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvclnm56LU_F3utmL79gVyEUkDy41UHws0H4n6pHH-Tzk-JohWK7sizXEDjpndix8DMFO4DyZLzJGnpG8kdCEP9un-svsAftOdNzEGYar3Bu6n4HiIXMkeivHf5_DF4GtfDye94zwNyVjbEg9x3-8qd9TxDjk7SsRITfSVrNCeZ3z5sfYMYDMTwyPBn1x6RIRG10tA1R6dwJ44HpkY5CQeu_2VY3EskuVzkJscgj0FzHTdOgHpKs2qYZs36Eq9IhjCd7SoWN3wlQuRAo0R_s4eAx3jnKigKKh1-Hwo2YwSSZ3K-jA16VQkdMfq2gSIL4DeGGXPmlJM5HzpRjmFHqrdI9CGhACQWecGUo4xHLkL0SXaJ-iVsDJ5r32yPi9PZCqTfExK1P59S_38IL4St1g45/4iw/GvkD_tYDTia9pk7Zb4zhuw/h1/h001.jN8QzYsv6DY079dBd_AgS1LFH0QrQxYuE0Awp7uV3uA) [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvclnm56LU_F3utmL79gVyEUkDy41UHws0H4n6pHH-TzvAHCxK25nzvTMuBk4Nrr37NFzK0tAWra_W65lN2tr8CSfSpC_hV9FNzWAcVd3S19ErPDEJh_nLjl6YdSLzbQ0adV0G5-6QewaoIct4f0686ZqL_nHI7mg4bcszKSamKNQASNHgtfgTf4aDmBRS6eThS9tjedgETZyJgclviHMgZNgSOX5-qemcj8SNqyASNKPMnCDm5Juy5SlcCa4l3kvHwrIKlL9fZgRCVaKRRk9ZantTXl3LWnuvXm9UALQMKO8lSG9hMgv4rw5a2-HrvS5tKcvYv72CziznLNhPbklrRg8rwew0ODrrwg6VQ5-lZ__peYEsCBSGdYd7XxGiau2anBUVTk0X9QUA48DFgGle9/4iw/GvkD_tYDTia9pk7Zb4zhuw/h2/h001.op0kKc-O5VmtnT1o3QuTk0VzEuTxhp2BXK_LGHGMIH0) | [DONATE](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvclnm56LU_F3utmL79gVyEUkDy41UHws0H4n6pHH-TzsqoBNDhihHYgm7JYsgThp-iIj2KvOW9qWd-BfUcHRvWNJbw_dWOEeHyimA9xQZkQ0Fv084NhbWEDkzx4daTu3tJDdiwHW9vWAvcmz5q7iUOitOypw4GRw2z62DPN6Yse_PoXFtX6lMWUOuGJaTcQbAyF4D--_2QxLaWMEubzrHbW91JHTrKb3TtuyYRnsq2IQlEaYk2VCeIl4iK71fA-ODncnpO2v-a2kpN6l4boV7PExfgjFAUNh_3Q_oiY6YVFqz9KHX78bNl09R_GyiR2R5GMYa4lkDBTQg3z900eOctO122FJuvg2kNV--W7zmGwELt-RWiQ_ri6tZ8dYaPTHSGak8As2_It1HfYIGKoYHM/4iw/GvkD_tYDTia9pk7Zb4zhuw/h3/h001.U2yAowxhmir-4FwaVAywrxMkxuw_n_HogxA7miolKw0)
---
**This Republican bill hands a $90,000 annual tax cut to households making over $1 million a year while working people get roughly $40.** Not $40 a day. $40 a year. That’s enough for a cup of coffee a month. And that’s before accounting for the impact of cuts to our healthcare and SNAP benefits.
In just over six months, they have begun dismantling programs that have taken generations to build. But we won’t be silent. We will continue to organize our communities, rally in the streets, and object to Trump and the GOP’s government of, by, and for the billionaires.

clean_body: This bill hands a $90,000 annual tax cut to households making over $1 million a year while working people get roughly $40. | | | Friend, Republicans are hoping that everyday people don’t know what’s in the Big Budget Betrayal bill. They better be ready for a rude awakening. I recently spoke with residents in my district to hear what they had to say about this disastrous new law. They called it "reverse Robin Hood." They said, "the billionaires need to share the wealth and stop being so greedy." And, when talking about how SNAP cuts are taking food from our kids, they said, "Why wouldn't you want our kids to be the biggest and the best?" The cuts Republicans made are so deep that 15 million people are expected to lose their health insurance and 51,000 people will die preventable deaths each year. And why? To fund trillions of dollars in tax cuts for the rich and corporations, and power Trump’s illegal, inhumane mass deportation scheme. Together, we’re saying hell no! | This Republican bill hands a $90,000 annual tax cut to households making over $1 million a year while working people get roughly $40. Not $40 a day. $40 a year. That’s enough for a cup of coffee a month. And that’s before accounting for the impact of cuts to our healthcare and SNAP benefits. In just over six months, they have begun dismantling programs that have taken generations to build. But we won’t be silent. We will continue to organize our communities, rally in the streets, and object to Trump and the GOP’s government of, by, and for the billionaires. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Together, we will never stop demanding a democracy for the people, rather than an oligarchy for Trump and his billionaire friends. In love and solidarity, Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . If you've ever felt like you're doing _all the right things,_ but life still isn't lining up the way you want...
I want to personally invite you to something that could shift everything.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ae816708-61b1-4213-9da7-b7266f38ba1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I'm speaking at the upcoming _**Rise Up Women!**_ **Master Your Mind and Mouth: Step Into Your Destiny** Summit, and it's designed to help you gain one key awareness:
**Your thoughts and your words are dictating your future.**
During this [free online event](https://substack.com/redirect/ae816708-61b1-4213-9da7-b7266f38ba1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), you'll learn how to step into your God-given destiny, starting with what you think and say every single day. You'll hear from a world-class, powerful lineup of speakers (myself included) on how to shift your mindset, upgrade your self-talk, and step into genuine transformation. 🩷
**It 's happening live from August 11th to August 15th at 8AM PT, and you can reserve your spot right here:**
[CLICK TO REGISTER](https://substack.com/redirect/ae816708-61b1-4213-9da7-b7266f38ba1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This could be the breakthrough you've been waiting for.
Excited to see you there!
* * *
Purchase My Book: [https://tinyurl.com/KathyBarnetteBook](https://substack.com/redirect/51cf8651-3638-46e8-9c86-5e38b23e3ff1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

subject: NEWSOM FIGHTS BACK >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
We need 100 top Democrats to sign on, ASAP!
| GAVIN NEWSOM IS FIGHTING BACK 👏 [](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=6&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O) Folks, this is HUGE: Trump & the GOP want to _rig the game_ so they can win in 2026, but Gavin Newsom won’t let them off the hook so easily!

**We’re thrilled that Gavin Newsom is FIGHTING BACK against MAGA gerrymandering in Texas — _but Trump & the GOP are FURIOUS._**

[**We’re URGENTLY rounding up urgent signatures to stand with Gavin Newsom in the face of MAGA attacks — Please, friend: Add your name to stand with Governor Gavin Newsom! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=7&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=8&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O)
---
TO: Governor Gavin Newsom
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. Dear Governor Newsom,

_Thank you so much for standing up for what’s right — even when it makes you a target for MAGA extremists!_

I refuse to sit on the sidelines while Trump attempts to STEAL the 2026 midterms.

**Thank goodness we have you leading the charge to FIGHT BACK!**

I’m doing everything in my power to protect Democrats like you who are brave enough to face Trump head-on and risk MAGA revenge.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice and WIN in 2026 »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=9&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=10&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O)
---
[](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/92921?t=11&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O)
**_X A grateful Democrat_**
\- The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=12&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20881%2E878179%2EbDNS5O)
---

In three months, New Jersey will head to the polls to elect its next governor.

And we have the chance to send a clear message: New Jersey deserves a leader who listens, and delivers. **That leader is Mikie Sherrill.**

[**This is your chance to directly support Mikie by splitting a donation between her campaign and EMILYs List:**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113041790/562449319/1606434486?refcode=WUEFH818_20250809_SherrillSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056784&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGJmODBkZC01Yjc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W7_MI89j1hHHTuk3Xxv_LO-z7XMkhfMxmsyU1JyT4BA=) | [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113041791/562449320/1606434486?refcode=WUEFH818_20250809_SherrillSplit_ALL_v1s1&sourceid=1056784&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGJmODBkZC01Yjc1LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=W7_MI89j1hHHTuk3Xxv_LO-z7XMkhfMxmsyU1JyT4BA=)

This summer, Mikie has been traveling across the Garden State, from town halls and coffee shops to union halls and transit stops, meeting with New Jerseyans and hearing directly about the issues that matter most to them and their families.

Her campaign wasn't built in a boardroom. It's built in community centers, on commuter trains, and in kitchen-table conversations with people who are ready for something better.

And Mikie's agenda reflects that.

**📱 She's ready to take on Big Tech** — Holding tech giants accountable for protecting children's safety online and ensuring that digital platforms serve the public good, not just their shareholders.

**🚆 She's committed to improving public transit** — Because New Jersey deserves buses and trains that are safe, reliable, and get you where you need to go, on time and without stress.

🏛️ **She's a fierce defender of reproductive freedom** — New Jersey's next governor will appoint two new state Supreme Court justices. That means the future of abortion access will be directly shaped by the person we elect in November. Mikie is a fierce pro-choice advocate with a strong record of defending reproductive freedom.

We know that the Trump administration is watching this race closely. Trump himself is determined to flip New Jersey with his hand-picked candidate, Jack Ciattarelli. But we have a candidate who's ready to prioritize her constituents over the whims of a despotic president. Mikie is ready to build a better future for New Jersey.

****[**When we elect Democratic pro-choice governors, progress follows. clean_body: Mikie Sherrill is listening and leading. Let's help her win. | = Peter, In three months, New Jersey will head to the polls to elect its next governor. And we have the chance to send a clear message: New Jersey deserves a leader who listens, and delivers. That leader is Mikie Sherrill. = | = This summer, Mikie has been traveling across the Garden State, from town halls and coffee shops to union halls and transit stops, meeting with New Jerseyans and hearing directly about the issues that matter most to them and their families. Her campaign wasn't built in a boardroom. It's built in community centers, on commuter trains, and in kitchen-table conversations with people who are ready for something better. And Mikie's agenda reflects that. 📱 She's ready to take on Big Tech — Holding tech giants accountable for protecting children's safety online and ensuring that digital platforms serve the public good, not just their shareholders. 🚆 She's committed to improving public transit — Because New Jersey deserves buses and trains that are safe, reliable, and get you where you need to go, on time and without stress. 🏛️ She's a fierce defender of reproductive freedom — New Jersey's next governor will appoint two new state Supreme Court justices. That means the future of abortion access will be directly shaped by the person we elect in November. Mikie is a fierce pro-choice advocate with a strong record of defending reproductive freedom. We know that the Trump administration is watching this race closely. Trump himself is determined to flip New Jersey with his hand-picked candidate, Jack Ciattarelli. But we have a candidate who's ready to prioritize her constituents over the whims of a despotic president. Mikie is ready to build a better future for New Jersey. **= = | = ---|--- = | = = | =** Thanks for helping to elect more women. – the EMILYs List Campaigns team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Brent Hennrich
subject: 13 years ago today was the start of the most painful journey of my life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITfdXTPGKAUZs9XXprPPSfkNJH2vBZpZgq3pQhyxvAW4M2FghokMzPiTW49Is8_gRgJZoIUhcRKFkysOTydmYQaB0I3ODnPbOoCLnyrcMXHpYm0APW4bmcleUpVl57p7dK8dzHZu1SfAyPm7sihGGB-ggEdeBkkny0HXnkspAQCR0AdP96R2zWMuKl2zQpRIeDFtdQLzvix3L2iCzXK-weEl_IxU9AduyXTyb2dpFTh8QzpjJ59at2LzO27GX3x6HZ/4iw/s8tS3f5BS7Gn3MFU0Lnh8A/h0/h001.jFIa2hmZ86KuG7QgLfYQH2a8GNMn_Mb3UNK_P3sq_D4)
---
Peter,

Thirteen years ago today I was overwhelmed with joy. My wife Amber and I found out we were expecting our second child. Our daughter Briana, just a year and a half herself, was over the moon at the idea of becoming a big sister. Our family was becoming more complete.
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUC3cdVOCiTvuFbqbhLs2RE0FH5UsK-qlrN6jy_Ypf1HEKSFpssNlURAHtlLPUK-jel7gyg4U8G-sdjI_EAhVAzejnNWSW0Rmw4_Ckan1H9pFw3tGpP8LdPGHKtTlxNGF_gBFeWDmjkWcZXkg-AfMBzlTo11G96SnKNtAUK0wgbXdG4RiwqTuMY0FJRm08U0tgT8qX7iVcViZV6TYB0gEcyy-KV4fBF1cVos1FyPmCXHo9FtJzJx23bvZo_lshzh6txxs3oRbgUjtl4GjIqQJME3M6z6cphBTVXRz9PjUNpp39nGzsMedcIR-GzL1Jd1v-D3OFyStQQkcMae7q7j2I0l/4iw/s8tS3f5BS7Gn3MFU0Lnh8A/h1/h001.1m2WJF9T0tVvJgkaF0Z2i6qYmSNdqC6k68fTzTSI6yk)
---
But just a few short weeks later, things took a dark turn. While I was in Alaska working for a family friend, Amber started bleeding in the middle of her shift at work. She was rushed to the Emergency Room and learned she was suffering from a placental abruption - a terrifying complication that turned our world upside down. Our marriage was still young, and now we faced juggling a toddler with a high-risk pregnancy, rushing to and from appointments with specialists, and an uncertain future. The doctors did everything they could for us. I did everything I could for Amber and our baby girl, whom we named Addison. We wanted this child. It was our choice to proceed with caution and hope. In the end, I had to make a split second decision - I had to choose to abort Addison to save Amber's life. It's the hardest decision I've ever made, and it was the right one. Even though it still hurts.
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUC3cdVOCiTvuFbqbhLs2RE0FH5UsK-qlrN6jy_Ypf1HEKSFpssNlURAHtlLPUK-jenbjdkgkrOy_4LFAFqgb-vDiQjrmsOlZLpHjMgf11pT6huB-N6-ekfFSzUNbePlq-NDFtoct4tglDAVX8q4j7fSxs3DiJWuRfvomQUstMqbbzGEhQCdT5SMUF2jaCIwXVmbm4WlmFnaWsmu4FT82gcqr7xWePXIvD9ut4ydWtBSjsADYyOc7rissz-IRKvz3Fy8Vcxfi-oesoPC1RK6JowZc_B5NzKDHiR-CVNIhG1TOYQ9dSK-iO4kVjVVCFPTkslniIp0PN8Z9Pak4J_MyLDt/4iw/s8tS3f5BS7Gn3MFU0Lnh8A/h2/h001.b5H6M8LDKpWXLJmi468zUojKCiF-TYR6zj5fnub5m1k)
---
Pregnancy is complicated and deeply personal. Complications like this happen all the time. Sometimes, they're much worse than what Amber went through. This was 13 years ago, in a state where abortion rights are firmly protected. And that is what it was - an abortion. In today's environment, in most U.S. states, that moment where I had to make the choice to save Amber's life would have been stalled by lawyers and lawmakers. Because when far-right politicians write their extreme abortion bans with so-called "exceptions" for "life of the mother" what they really mean is they get to decide whether or not my wife is really at risk in that split second while her life hangs in the balance. Not my choice. Not Amber's choice. Theirs. No one should have to go through what our family endured without access to compassionate, expert care. No one should lose the right to make decisions about their own body, their own family, their own future. For me, the question of abortion rights is not a question at all. There is zero room for compromise on this issue. You cannot make concessions with people who would put their choice to risk YOUR family's future over your own right to choose. [**When I 'm in Congress I will fight like a junkyard dog for abortion rights and bodily autonomy.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUC3cdVOCiTvuFbqbhLs2RE0FH5UsK-qlrN6jy_Ypf1HEKSFpssNlURAHtlLPUK-jelfCj6ddT53CJpH2QJAKuPQeZ4rexZQWH02MAGpI9d-f14LGQSDmEaMLqvWQLqap2rWIsUT9w5wUcQWxxkpH3_GpUTvDDGsv2uWeGkEhbtavj4E8W9sCG3Kjt0SBqUy80ZVhZBp_3fcrOdR9g9kkcvyUofq3uVoJME1i6kZJyE69o05lI_8xWvko81GvYQFkp1fiHIafbUTmp6ZySx0igQVcyrFzmtDkaBIRW4o_G3Q5HbJZL1BJKHfQlkOKuwLzXL_DAzbWDJ4rCKp7VAJT1fi/4iw/s8tS3f5BS7Gn3MFU0Lnh8A/h3/h001.Nz1hnieECOUifLrlKXuv4CC4XEhHO3W4gLp9rmRdrsg) I will fight like hell to protect access, choice, and dignity - no compromises, no backing down. We can't afford politicians who waffle, who look the other way, or who surreptitiously vote for abortion restrictions to appear "moderate" to right-wing extremists. If I had not been able to make the decision I did, in the exact moment it had to be made, I would have been left a single dad with a toddler. Briana would've grown up without her mother. My babies Braydon and Brynlee never would have been born. Our family would be incomplete. It's not policy. It's personal. [**Abortion is healthcare, and healthcare is a human right. If you agree, then join our campaign and help me fight for every woman 's right to control her own body, and every family to be control of their own healthcare decisions.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUC3cdVOCiTvuFbqbhLs2RE0FH5UsK-qlrN6jy_Ypf1HEKSFpssNlURAHtlLPUK-jekwkEfS0QsPVT_XmHJzHCvYOZN559wUQzmZnv0NRAnqu1JHKpi_QRUe63tsy_t75Pikk0RR1LcP3--r-P4hNzkEMSHzpjLNJrG9YM5mLm3Qu2WfgomFeynLRC4O0l_EYIUs_RG32CqZfAdKAgZ-s1Ves4qJlyfFhSTIT3ReIN9XMAsPUVOtpMquaWcNo5UemVdbrg_MVPU_NduGUJ4myV2qI4k5J99pqxF5E4u3zv_cCbGPmMVJO3RANuO_wEmmF8DMthConZtUofXjOiRUwgVV/4iw/s8tS3f5BS7Gn3MFU0Lnh8A/h4/h001.ZGzVpcEknWtYFfAfnnwUA4y5ufslYyy-7yVB4F30A5Q)
Let's Heal Washington!

Brent Hennrich

clean_body: | | Peter, Thirteen years ago today I was overwhelmed with joy. My wife Amber and I found out we were expecting our second child. Our daughter Briana, just a year and a half herself, was over the moon at the idea of becoming a big sister. Our family was becoming more complete. | But just a few short weeks later, things took a dark turn. While I was in Alaska working for a family friend, Amber started bleeding in the middle of her shift at work. She was rushed to the Emergency Room and learned she was suffering from a placental abruption - a terrifying complication that turned our world upside down. Our marriage was still young, and now we faced juggling a toddler with a high-risk pregnancy, rushing to and from appointments with specialists, and an uncertain future. The doctors did everything they could for us. I did everything I could for Amber and our baby girl, whom we named Addison. We wanted this child. It was our choice to proceed with caution and hope. In the end, I had to make a split second decision - I had to choose to abort Addison to save Amber's life. It's the hardest decision I've ever made, and it was the right one. Even though it still hurts. | Pregnancy is complicated and deeply personal. Complications like this happen all the time. Sometimes, they're much worse than what Amber went through. This was 13 years ago, in a state where abortion rights are firmly protected. And that is what it was - an abortion. In today's environment, in most U.S. states, that moment where I had to make the choice to save Amber's life would have been stalled by lawyers and lawmakers. Because when far-right politicians write their extreme abortion bans with so-called "exceptions" for "life of the mother" what they really mean is they get to decide whether or not my wife is really at risk in that split second while her life hangs in the balance. Not my choice. Not Amber's choice. Theirs. No one should have to go through what our family endured without access to compassionate, expert care. No one should lose the right to make decisions about their own body, their own family, their own future. For me, the question of abortion rights is not a question at all. There is zero room for compromise on this issue. You cannot make concessions with people who would put their choice to risk YOUR family's future over your own right to choose. I will fight like hell to protect access, choice, and dignity - no compromises, no backing down. We can't afford politicians who waffle, who look the other way, or who surreptitiously vote for abortion restrictions to appear "moderate" to right-wing extremists. If I had not been able to make the decision I did, in the exact moment it had to be made, I would have been left a single dad with a toddler. Briana would've grown up without her mother. My babies Braydon and Brynlee never would have been born. Our family would be incomplete. It's not policy. It's personal. Let's Heal Washington! Brent Hennrich | | Marie Gluesenkamp-Perez has betrayed Democrats and the voters of WA-3 by repeatedly voting with MAGA against women's rights, immigrants, and more. Brent Hennrich is a lifelong Washingtonian, a proud working class dad, and lifelong Democratic activist. Chip in now to help elect a Democrat ready to stand up to MAGA. | | | | Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington ---|--- (C)2025 Brent Hennrich for Washington, all rights reserved Our mailing address is: Brent Hennrich for Washington PO BOX 820070 Vancouver, WA 98682 We hate to see you go, but if you believe you received this email in error, or you no longer wish to receive our emails you can | | Sent via . | | **_Meet Russell:_** Born and raised in Surfside Beach, SC, Congressman Russell Fry is the proud and homegrown Representative for South Carolina’s 7th Congressional District. Growing up in a cinder block home with no heating or air conditioning, Russell learned at an early age that hard work and perseverance pave the only true path to the American Dream. He watched his parents work day in and day out to make ends meet. And the values they instilled in him during his upbringing led Russell to self-fund both his undergraduate education at the University of South Carolina, and later his law studies at the Charleston School of Law. At Charleston, Russell served as the President of the Student Bar Association and even helped the school achieve its American Bar Association accreditation. He went on to practice law in the Grand Strand area for over a decade before entering the political arena to serve in the South Carolina House of Representatives for State House District 106 (Horry County). During his seven-year tenure in the SC House, Russell fought to safeguard Constitutional liberties, lower taxes, and put the best interests of South Carolina families above all else. He climbed the ranks to become Chief Majority Whip, and also chaired the House Opioid Prevention Study Committee. Under Russell’s leadership, 18 policy initiatives to combat the opioid crisis were signed into law. Since the first day he stepped foot in Washington, Russell has strived to shake up the corrupt status quo and deliver real change for the American people. He believes adamantly that so long as the American people stand united, we can keep the American Dream alive for every citizen of our great nation. Russell is a dedicated husband to his wife, Bronwen, and a loving father to his son, James. The family lives in Murrells Inlet with their chocolate lab, Jasper.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Trump’s “no tax on tips” plan is a scam
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Trump’s tip tax law isn’t what it seems. Let’s fight back.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250809-5321-footer&t=1&akid=5321%2E420207%2Ek8GryU)
Peter,
Donald Trump wants you to think he’s helping workers.
**But his no tax on tips plan? It’s a scam.**
It offers little real benefit to the workers who need relief most, while handing _billions_ to the wealthy. It’s a distraction — designed to look helpful, but hiding a massive giveaway to the rich.
**As an organizer and progressive leader in Congress, I’ve always fought for real solutions to help poor and working people** — like Medicare for All, a wealth tax, and the Green New Deal — not headline bait and handouts for billionaires.
But to keep leading this fight, I need your help. Powering this campaign and the year-round organizing work we do takes resources, and we rely on grassroots donors like you to help us keep it up.
[**If you’re with me, pitch in $3 or more today to help me keep holding the line for working people.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250809-2?refcode=em20250809-5321&amount=3&refcode2=5321_420207_k8GryU&akid=5321%2E420207%2Ek8GryU)
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20250809-2?amount=3&refcode=em20250809-5321&refcode2=5321_420207_k8GryU&akid=5321%2E420207%2Ek8GryU)**
Let’s show Trump and his billionaire backers that we’re not falling for it, and we’re not backing down. We don’t need more distractions — we need action.
In solidarity,
Pramila
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250809-5321-footer&t=2&refcode2=5321_420207_k8GryU&akid=5321%2E420207%2Ek8GryU)**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

# [Of Canoes and Caesars: J.D. Vance's Imperial Birthday Float](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=170552946&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwNTUyOTQ2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ3NzI5MDcsImV4cCI6MTc1NzM2NDkwNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.erKJTapf5VdWNGHXFnj6UAwrZOJfgKNXkX6-WNK60BA)
### The pampered prince of political prostitution enjoys the spoils of betraying everything he ever said he believed
| | [DavidShuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)
---
| Aug 9
---
| [](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)

### _Folks, we 've put Five Figures Into the Soundcheck Fund. Your Contribution of $1.51/wk--via[ a Paid Subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/79d37f59-2b71-4f20-b180-3ddd4b0fee6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--Supports its Goal to Grow Independent Media. Please Join us--Consider Becoming a [Paid Subscriber](https://substack.com/redirect/79d37f59-2b71-4f20-b180-3ddd4b0fee6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to Blue Amp _
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.thixqaQnNfx3UEPM7yyyAWCBQPrLsDj9GjoMrHjT1po?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=170552946)
**By David Shuster**
We are now a nation governed not by leaders, but by increasingly cartoonish simulations animated more by vanity than virtue, more by stagecraft than statecraft. And there is no better mascot for this decline and rot than Vice President J.D. Vance, the Appalachian Horatio Alger turned Beltway embarrassment.
In his latest escapade, Vance demanded river waters rise in homage to his kayak.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6dd69531-a104-438b-ad7f-07214ab25664?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Yes, the Vice President--whom the Almighty (clearly in jest) saw fit to give both ambition and a memoir--marked his 41st birthday not with reflection or restraint, but with an aquatic pageant [worthy of Nero](https://substack.com/redirect/18c36f95-94bc-4d41-adf2-6f7de9fb8f59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
On August 2nd, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, that obedient regiment of hydraulic butlers, received a curious request: lower the dam [at the aptly named Caeser ](https://substack.com/redirect/b8a90807-950c-4caa-91b7-ae5b61d9817a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Creek Lake, raise the flow of the Little Miami River, and ensure the Vice President and his Secret Service flotilla could glide through southwestern Ohio without so much as a puddle to inconvenience them.
Officially, the request was for the safe navigation of Vance's security detail. But multiple sources in and out of government report the [whole charade was to make the river nice and deep for Vance's little birthday paddle](https://substack.com/redirect/ac6f6ebf-be5f-43a7-a5a1-a2815facbaf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). One authority said that deepening the river provided Vance with "ideal kayaking conditions."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7a8495ea-dc9d-4581-b9d0-f12fc265e4a4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[The locals, of course, were never consulted](https://substack.com/redirect/5f658ce2-6054-4c31-8092-482883165713?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Residents along the lake saw the water vanish from their docks; folks along the Little Miami River were never asked whether their weekend fishing trip should be sacrificed for His Excellency's joyride.
All of it, naturally, was paid for by the American taxpayer, that perennial chump who gets to fund the gluttony of the powerful while being scolded for needing food stamps.
Clearly it is not enough that we pay for the Vice President's motorcade, his security detail, his gourmet lunches, his speechwriters, and his self-mythologizing PR team. Now we must rearrange the landscape itself-- including lowering lakes and filling rivers so Vance can cosplay for a few hours as Huck Finn.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7950cf9c-113b-411c-9c3f-6fcff3f2f730?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One might expect such a stunt in a Third World banana republic. Or perhaps in an oil drenched monarchy led by a Prince with a strong attachment to personal aqueducts.
But this is in the United States of America.
And we have a Vice President treating a public river like it's his own personal bathtub. This is not leadership. This is not even politics. [This is sheer, unvarnished narcissism](https://substack.com/redirect/0e439d0f-c642-442f-b415-3a6613b8d2b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
What makes the whole incident even more nauseating is Vance's history. He has claimed to be the voice of the forgotten American, the bard of the Rust Belt, the memoirist of misery.
"Hillbilly Elegy," he called it--a book filled with melodrama and meager insight, wherein Vance explained that the working class suffers not from economic abandonment, but from personal failure and bad morals. The elites, Vance wrote, are smug and out of touch.
And now here is Vance, quite literally redirecting rivers for his own amusement.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d948748f-6b7f-40d8-bb39-293760ce9150?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ethics watchdogs in both parties are aghast. One former Bush administration official described Vance's river stunt as "hypocritical and outrageous." An Obama-era counsel said no previous administration would "have ever permitted this."
In true Roman fashion,[ Vance's office denies all wrongdoing](https://substack.com/redirect/00823460-1131-485b-9a1f-993a8416d498?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "Standard coordination," they say. "Nothing out of the ordinary." Of course not.
It is now standard procedure for federal agencies to adjust topography at the whims of elected narcissists. It is [standard for public assets to serve private indulgence](https://substack.com/redirect/6755968f-a5fe-4cab-b75e-e32b781dcf0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It is standard to humiliate the republic for the sake of optics. All of it is standard.
That's the problem.
Our country has endured horrific men before who were a heartbeat away from the Presidency. But rarely has the United States been forced to endure a VP combination as putrid as J.D. Vance's self-pity, self-regard, and self-service--all wrapped in a soggy birthday kayak.
If there were any justice, the Little Miami River would have risen up not in homage, but in protest, flipping over Vance's kayak and depositing J.D. into the water and mud.
That is where bottom feeders like him belong.
Whether on a river or in the halls of power, J.D. Vance is dirty, slimy, and self-absorbed.
* * *
_David Shuster is an Emmy award winning broadcast journalist who is best known for his work at NBC News and as an anchor MSNBC. He is a contributor to Blue Amp Substack and co-host of[our weekly show (Thursdays, 2pm et, on Substack), "Amped Up w/ Cliff Schecter."](https://substack.com/redirect/acbe4826-5a7d-4447-b14c-294167fbb353?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_

In true Roman fashion,. "Standard coordination," they say. "Nothing out of the ordinary." Of course not. It is now standard procedure for federal agencies to adjust topography at the whims of elected narcissists. It is . It is standard to humiliate the republic for the sake of optics. All of it is standard. That's the problem. Our country has endured horrific men before who were a heartbeat away from the Presidency. But rarely has the United States been forced to endure a VP combination as putrid as J.D. Vance's self-pity, self-regard, and self-service--all wrapped in a soggy birthday kayak. If there were any justice, the Little Miami River would have risen up not in homage, but in protest, flipping over Vance's kayak and depositing J.D. into the water and mud. That is where bottom feeders like him belong. Whether on a river or in the halls of power, J.D. Vance is dirty, slimy, and self-absorbed. * David Shuster is an Emmy award winning broadcast journalist who is best known for his work at NBC News and as an anchor MSNBC. He is a contributor to Blue Amp Substack and co-host of Folks, we 've put Five Figures Into the Soundcheck Fund. Your Contribution of $1.51/wk-- its Goal to Grow Independent Media. Please Join us--Consider Becoming a to Blue Amp Thank you. And thanks for all you do for democracy. Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Blue Amp, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?
